--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/css-instructions.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets
// FILE:      css-instructions.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/css-instructions.md
------------------------------------------------------------------
Prompt / Documentation: The RaggieSoft Theming Architecture
Core Philosophy: "State Drives Style." The site does not load a static CSS file. Instead, header.php dynamically constructs a load queue based on two variables set in the Router (index.php): $site and $theme.

1. Environment & Asset Paths (Crucial)
The file paths you see in the code are dynamic. You must ensure your environment is set up correctly for assets to load.

Development (Local): Assets live in the local folder /raggiesoft-assets/.

Production (Live): Assets are served via CDN from https://assets.raggiesoft.com/.

CORS Warning: If you set up a new domain (e.g., raggiesoftknox.com), you MUST update the CORS rules in the DigitalOcean Spaces settings for the assets.raggiesoft.com bucket, or fonts and scripts will fail to load.

2. The CSS Loading Order (The Cascade)
The header loads files in this specific order. Later files override earlier ones.

bootstrap-base.css (Common): The raw Bootstrap 5 framework. Never touch this.

root.css (Theme): The Foundation. Defines :root variables (colors, fonts, RGB values). This paints the broad strokes.

extras.css (Theme): The FX Engine. Contains custom classes for visual flair (e.g., .tome-container, .text-glow). It uses the variables defined in root.css.

bootstrap-header.css (Common): Global layout for the navbar.

bootstrap-footer.css (Common): Global layout for the footer and the sticky audio player.

safety-net.css (Theme): The Override. Used to force specific elements (like Navbars or Buttons) to behave when the default Bootstrap styles clash with your theme.

raggiesoft-extras.css (Common): Global hotfixes that apply to the entire network.

3. How to Construct a New Theme
To build a new visual identity (e.g., "The Silver Gauntlet"), you need to create a folder in your local raggiesoft-assets repository and define three critical files.

Step 1: Create the Directory

Site-Level Theme: raggiesoft-assets/[site-name]/css/bootstrap/

Example: raggiesoft-assets/aethel/css/bootstrap/

Sub-Theme (Context Override): raggiesoft-assets/[site-name]/css/bootstrap/[theme-name]/

Example: raggiesoft-assets/aethel/css/bootstrap/gloom/

Step 2: Create the Three Files

root.css (The Palette)

Purpose: Define your colors and fonts here.

Must Have: A :root block, plus overrides for [data-bs-theme="light"] and [data-bs-theme="dark"].

Key Tip: Always define RGB versions of your colors (e.g., --bs-primary-rgb) so Bootstrap's opacity utilities work.

extras.css (The Look)

Purpose: Custom CSS classes that define the "vibe" (glassmorphism, parchment textures, neon glows).

Key Tip: Use the variables you defined in root.css so you can change colors easily later.

safety-net.css (The Fix)

Purpose: Brute-force overrides using !important. Use this to ensure text is readable on your background or to force the Navbar to a specific color regardless of light/dark mode.

Key Tip: If the header looks wrong, fix it here.

4. How to Activate a Theme
You do not link CSS files manually. You set the context in the Router (public/index.php).

Example: Activating the "Gloom" Theme

PHP

'/library/aethel/book/chapter-1' => [
    'title' => 'Chapter 1',
    
    // 1. Sets the Asset Folder (loads /aethel/css/bootstrap/...)
    'site'  => 'aethel', 
    
    // 2. Sets the Sub-Folder (loads /aethel/css/bootstrap/gloom/...)
    // If null, it loads the default files in the site folder.
    'theme' => 'gloom',  
    
    'view'  => 'pages/library/aethel/chapter-1',
],
Common Pitfall: If you set 'theme' => 'gloom', you MUST have root.css, extras.css, AND safety-net.css inside the gloom folder. If one is missing, the header will try to load it and fail, potentially breaking the cascade.

His backpack is strapped to the back of his chair, containing the shards of his destroyed AAC device. He has no voice and no plan, except for a couple of crumpled dollar bills in his wallet—money meant for vending machines that is now his only hope for fare on the **TAPS** (Transit Authority of the Puget Sound) bus to the airport.\r\n\r\n**The \"Rootkit Key\":** This song captures the trauma so perfectly that it becomes a permanent, override-proof trigger for Leo. In later years, hearing just the opening notes will bypass his logic centers and cause an immediate autistic meltdown. This reaction is absolute: even if he is **actively being grounded** by all three women (Luna, Olivia, and Stella) and even if they **warn him** the song is about to play, the trigger cannot be stopped.\r\n\r\n**LYRICS:**\r\n\r\n[Intro]\r\n(Starts with the sound of cold, pouring rain and a deep, pulsing synthesizer bass. A terrifying moment of vulnerability where Leo has to communicate his needs to strangers without words.\r\n* **The Transfer:** The kindness of strangers: the Route 164 bus operator explained Leo's situation to their dispatcher over the radio, who then radioed ahead to the Route 161 bus to have them be prepared for Leo's arrival.\r\n* **Leg 2:** The **161** from the station to **SEA-TAC Airport**.\r\n\r\nThe core emotional beat of this track is the **kindness of strangers**. Unlike Delores, the bus operators don't scream at him. They see a young man in a wheelchair, soaking wet, holding a broken screen, and terrified. They take the time to read his desperate, hand-signed gestures. They secure his chair. They get him to the right stop. It is the first humanity he has experienced in weeks.\r\n\r\n**LYRICS:**\r\n\r\n[Instrumental]\r\n\r\n(The track opens with the pneumatic hiss of bus doors opening—a sound that used to signal entrapment, but now signals escape.)\r\n\r\n(A steady, forward-moving bassline kicks in at 110 BPM. He finds a TTY payphone bank. Having only ever used modern AAC devices, the archaic teletypewriter is alien to him. He doesn't know the protocols—\"GA\" (Go Ahead) or \"SK\" (Stop Keying).\r\n\r\nDesperate, he dials `0` and types a clumsy request for an emergency collect call to the one number burned into his memory since age 14: his home.\r\n\r\nThe track captures the agonizing wait as the Relay Operator connects the line. The emotional climax hits not with a musical crescendo, but with a single spoken word sampled from the \"Black Box\" recording of the call: **Stella accepting the charges.**\r\n\r\n**LYRICS:**\r\n\r\n[Instrumental]\r\n\r\n(The track opens with the harsh, rhythmic *skree-skree* of a TTY handshake connection. He ignores the \"GA\" prompts, just typing a continuous stream of panic.)\r\n\r\n(The music drops to a tense silence, broken only by the \"ringing\" tone.)\r\n\r\n[Spoken Sample: Relay Operator]\r\n(Heavily processed, sounding like a distant transmission)\r\n\"I have a collect call from... Leo. The nightmare is over.)\r\n\r\n[Outro]\r\n(The sound of rapid typing—Leo pouring his heart out via HCO (Hearing Carry Over)—blends with the warm synth, fading into the next track.)"

Leo has made it through TSA. The threat of Delores is physically behind the security checkpoint. Now, the healing begins.\r\n\r\n**1. The Restoration:** Olivia takes Leo to a family restroom—not to scream at him, but to restore his dignity. She helps him change out of his soaked, filthy clothes into a brand new, dry sweatshirt and sweatpants she bought at a terminal shop. The sensory relief of dry cotton on his skin is treated as a religious experience in the lyrics.\r\n\r\n**2. The \"Anti-Pasco\" Moment:** The pivotal scene occurs when Olivia needs to use the restroom herself. Knowing Leo is too terrified to be left alone, she asks him a question Delores never did: **\"Is this okay?\"** She asks for his permission. It is a small act that returns a massive amount of agency to him.\r\n\r\n**3. The View:** The track ends with them sitting by the window, Leo’s wheelchair positioned so he can watch the planes take off.     "album": "Static & Silence",
    "url": "C:/Program Files/Git/engine-room/artists/mirage/albums/2001-static-and-silence",
    "type": "track",
    "content": "**LORE NOTE:**\r\nThe album's great irony.\r\n\r\nMost of the world knows this song as the massive holiday hit by **The Arctic Symphony Project** (a pseudonym Mirage used for their 2002 album *Northern Star Requiem*). That version—soaring, orchestral, and festive—was the soundtrack to the winter of 2002 and was still in heavy rotation when Leo sat in SEA-TAC Airport in **November 2003**. Hearing that bright, hopeful version over the terminal PA is what gave him the strength to believe he might actually make it home.\r\n\r\nBut on *this* album (released 2001), we hear the **original**: dark, moody, and desperate. This is the song as it was written in the midst of the trauma—a heavy, industrial prayer for rescue.\r\n\r\n**Label Note:** Engine Room Records did not mandate the holiday album. Julian and Maya chose to re-record the song in 2002 as an act of reclamation—transforming their darkest moment into a source of pure, commercial joy.\r\n\r\n**LYRICS:**\r\n\r\n[Intro]\r\n(Starts with a dramatic, slightly melancholic piano melody, soon joined by swelling orchestral strings that build anticipation)\r\n\r\n[Verse 1]\r\nA thousand miles of winter gray\r\nAnother dawn, another day\r\nThe engine's hum, a weary soul\r\nA heavy price, a heavy toll\r\nI see your face on frozen glass\r\nA memory that's built to last\r\nA single light that I must find\r\nTo leave the endless dark behind\r\n\r\n[Chorus]\r\nAnd the lights are bright, on this winter's night\r\nBecause you're coming home\r\nYes, you're finally coming home\r\nThrough the wind and rain, through the doubt and pain\r\nYou are not alone\r\nNo, you're finally coming home!\r\n\r\n[Verse 2]\r\nThe towns fly by, a blur of light\r\nI count the hours in the night\r\nRemembering a whispered vow\r\nIt feels so close, it's coming now\r\nThe fire's lit, the table's set\r\nA promise that I haven't met\r\nBut in my heart, the feeling grows\r\nA warmth that melts the winter snows\r\n\r\n[Chorus]\r\nAnd the lights are bright, on this winter's night\r\nBecause you're coming home\r\nYes, you're finally coming home\r\nThrough the wind and rain, through the doubt and pain\r\nYou are not alone\r\nNo, you're finally coming home!\r\n\r\n[Instrumental Bridge]\r\n(Orchestra swells, strings become more frantic and dramatic)\r\n\r\n[Guitar Solo]\r\n(A powerful, melodic, and emotional electric guitar solo soars over the orchestra, telling the story of the struggle and the breakthrough)\r\n\r\n[Chorus]\r\nAnd the lights are bright, on this winter's night\r\nBecause you're coming home\r\nYes, you're finally coming home\r\nThrough the wind and rain, through the doubt and pain\r\nYou are not alone\r\nNo, you're finally coming home!\r\n\r\n[Outro]\r\n(Music becomes even more grand and layered with powerful choral vocals)\r\nComing home!\r\n(Finally coming home!)\r\nOn this day!\r\n(The orchestra and guitar build to a final, triumphant chord and fade)"

    "id": "2-07-a-song-of-homecoming",
    "title": "A Song of Homecoming",
    "artist": "Mirage",
    "album": "Static & Silence",
    "url": "C:/Program Files/Git/engine-room/artists/mirage/albums/2001-static-and-silence",
    "type": "track",
    "content": "**LORE NOTE:**\r\nA driving, mid-tempo rock anthem that balances anxiety with ascending hope.\r\n\r\nThe setting is the **Gate Area** at SEA-TAC. Leo and Olivia are waiting for **Gateway Airlines**. Because the airline has no assigned seating, a chaotic, aggressive queue is forming, triggering Leo's sensory overload. He braces himself to be crushed in the rush, terrified he will be separated from Olivia.\r\n\r\n**The Pre-Boarding Revelation:**\r\nLeo doesn't know that his disability gives him the right to pre-board. He expects a fight. Instead, Olivia calmly guides him past the line. The Gate Agent doesn't stop them; she smiles and waves them through before anyone else. The realization that he has rights—that he isn't just cargo—is the spark that finally ignites the \"Homecoming\" chorus.\r\n\r\n**The Internal Conflict:**\r\nLeo holds his boarding pass with a death grip. His \"Painted Smile\" mask is still partially engaged; his eyes dart around the terminal, half-expecting Delores (Vanessa) to burst through security and drag him back. He cannot fully believe he is leaving until the wheels actually leave the ground.\r\n\r\n**The Sound:**\r\nThe track features a nervous, staccato guitar riff (the anxiety of the crowd) that slowly opens up into a wide, majestic chorus (the quiet safety of the jetway). The \"Industrial\" noise of the city is still present in the verses—hissing steam, mechanical clatter—but the chorus is pure, sweeping 80s optimism, representing Olivia's steady presence guiding him home.\r\n\r\n**LYRICS:**\r\n\r\n[Verse 1]\r\nThe monitor flickers, the crowd begins to sway\r\nI'm bracing for the crush, the price I have to pay\r\nThe noise is getting louder, the air is getting thin\r\nI'm terrified of losing you amidst the din\r\nBut then you touch my shoulder, you guide me past the line\r\n\"We're going first,\" you whisper, \"This moment is all mine\"\r\nThe agent scans the paper, the heavy door swings wide\r\nLeaving all the chaos and the angry noise outside\r\n\r\n[Chorus]\r\nAnd I hear a song of homecoming\r\nUnderneath the fear, a steady humming\r\nA melody that's calling me away\r\nTo the light of a brand new day\r\nIt's faint and fragile, but it's true\r\nI'm walking down this hall with you\r\nA song of homecoming...\r\nPlease let it be true...\r\n\r\n[Verse 2]\r\nThe tunnel is so quiet, the carpet soft and gray\r\nA silence that I haven't heard for many, many days\r\nI check behind me, trembling, just one last time\r\nSearching for the punishment, searching for the crime\r\nBut there's only empty hallway and the echo of our feet\r\nAnd the promise of a window seat\r\nYou help me to the transfer, you buckle me in tight\r\nFor the first time in forever, I'm ready for the flight\r\n\r\n[Chorus]\r\nAnd I hear a song of homecoming\r\nUnderneath the fear, a steady humming\r\nA melody that's calling me away\r\nTo the light of a brand new day\r\nIt's faint and fragile, but it's true\r\nI'm walking down this hall with you\r\nA song of homecoming...\r\nPlease let it be true...\r\n\r\n[Bridge]\r\n(The music swells. The industrial clatter fades completely, replaced by the rising, high-pitched whine of jet turbines spooling up.)\r\nThe cabin pressure, the safety sign\r\nFor the first time in months, this life is mine\r\nYou take my hand, you squeeze it tight\r\nWe're lifting off into the night!\r\n\r\n[Outro]\r\n(The drums kick into a double-time beat—the takeoff roll.)\r\nWe're going home!\r\nWe're going home!\r\nDon't look back!\r\nWe're going home!\r\n(Fade out on the roar of engines)"

He reclines his seat, lowers the tray table, and curls into Olivia's shoulder. Today is the first time he has been touched with genuine, non-transactional love since the nightmare began.\r\n\r\n**LYRICS:**\r\n\r\n[Verse 1]\r\nThe engine hums a steady beat\r\nThirty thousand tired feet\r\nAbove the clouds, a sea of white\r\nEscaping from the endless night\r\nYour hand in mine, a steady grace\r\nThe only anchor in this place\r\nYou let me lean against your side\r\nWith nothing left to run or hide\r\n\r\n[Chorus]\r\n(Music swells with a warm, gentle, and melodic feel)\r\nAnd for the first time in so long, I can breathe\r\nA quiet promise, I believe\r\nYour shoulder is my only home right now\r\nA silent, true, and sacred vow\r\nThis sanctuary in the sky\r\nBeneath a calm and endless eye\r\nIs all I need to feel okay\r\nAnd chase the lingering fog away\r\n\r\n[Verse 2]\r\nI think about the static sound\r\nOn that unholy, hollow ground\r\nI think about the pouring rain\r\nThe endless fear, the burning pain\r\nBut then I feel you squeeze my hand\r\nAnd I begin to understand\r\nThe storm has passed, the war is done\r\nA quiet battle has been won\r\n\r\n[Chorus]\r\n(Music swells with a warm, gentle, and melodic feel)\r\nAnd for the first time in so long, I can breathe\r\nA quiet promise, I believe\r\nYour shoulder is my only home right now\r\nA silent, true, and sacred vow\r\nThis sanctuary in the sky\r\nBeneath a calm and endless eye\r\nIs all I need to feel okay\r\nAnd chase the lingering fog away\r\n\r\n[Instrumental Bridge]\r\n(A melodic, slightly melancholic, but ultimately hopeful synthesizer solo, with clean, arpeggiated notes that sound like data flowing before fading out.)\r\n\r\n[Outro]\r\n(Music fades to just a low, humming drone, like the cabin of the plane. No screaming, no running, no \"Pasco Lock\" moments. Just Olivia guiding him calmly to the next gate, buying him a magazine, and boarding the plane.\r\n\r\nThe music reflects this new reality. The \"Industrial\" grit of Disc 1 is completely purged from the mix, replaced by clean, arpeggiated synthesizers and a steady, propulsive 125 BPM beat. It is the sound of a plane cutting through the night sky, carrying Leo back to the only people who truly know him. The mood is no longer about *escaping*; it is about *arriving*.\r\n\r\n**LYRICS:**\r\n\r\n[Instrumental]\r\n\r\n(The track opens with a clean, rapid electronic drum beat at 125 BPM—fast, eager, and light.)\r\n\r\n(A warm, melodic synthesizer bass line enters, driving the song forward with a sense of purpose. It is a \"System Crash\" born of the unbearable reality that he is actually safe.\r\n\r\n**The Promise:**\r\nThe most critical moment of the entire rock opera occurs in the second verse. **Luna (Isabella Croft)** delivers the line that finally breaks the trauma loop: **\"Your broken heart will mend with time.\"**\r\n\r\nThis isn't a platitude. It is a binding vow from sister to brother. It is Luna using her voice to overwrite the damage Delores did, promising him that his current pain is temporary, but his family is permanent. When she sings this line, the \"Painted Smile\" mask doesn't just fall; it shatters, and Leo finally allows himself to be held.\r\n\r\n**LYRICS:**\r\n\r\n[Intro]\r\n(The track opens with a wall of chaotic, industrial noise—distorted static, rushing wind, and a frantic, irregular heartbeat. The industrial gloom vanishes, replaced by \"Hair Metal\" drums, soaring vocals, and the joy of a open highway.\r\n\r\nFor Leo, this isn't just a song; it is the sound of his authentic self returning. Now, after surviving the industrial nightmare of Disc 1, he returns to that melody with a profound, weary gratitude.\r\n\r\nHe realizes that his sisters and best friend weren't just \"guiding\" him; they were the only things that kept him alive. The lyrics explicitly reference the trauma mechanisms he developed (\"I learned to wear a painted smile / And walk a cold and lonely mile\"), acknowledging the scars that will remain. But the music—a calm, sophisticated 80s pop arrangement featuring a soulful saxophone—signals that while the innocence is gone, the safety is permanent. He vows never to leave the Fortress again.\r\n\r\n**LYRICS:**\r\n\r\n[Verse 1]\r\nThere was a boy I used to be\r\nWho saw the world so simply\r\nThought every road was straight and true\r\nBeneath a sky of perfect blue\r\nHe thought that love was just a phrase\r\nFor sunny, easy, golden days\r\nHe called you all his guiding stars\r\nBefore he knew about the scars\r\n\r\n[Chorus]\r\nBut I learned the sky can fall away\r\nAnd turn a bright and sunny day to gray\r\nAnd in the static and the night\r\nYou were my only guiding light\r\nMore than a feeling, more than a name\r\nYou were the fire, you were the flame\r\nThat led me through the pouring rain\r\nAnd brought me back to life again\r\n\r\n[Verse 2]\r\nI learned to wear a painted smile\r\nAnd walk a cold and lonely mile\r\nTo build a wall of industrial sound\r\nOn cold and unfamiliar ground\r\nI learned how silence has a weight\r\nAnd how a heart can fill with hate\r\nI learned that trust can be a lie\r\nBeneath a cold and empty sky\r\n\r\n[Bridge]\r\nTwo stars are here, held close and tight\r\nOne shines a warm and distant light\r\nA friendly face on a glowing screen\r\nThe miles can't ever come between\r\nThe bond we made, the truth we know\r\nHelping a broken boy to grow\r\n\r\n[Chorus]\r\nAnd when the sky would fall away\r\nAnd turn a bright and sunny day to gray\r\nAnd in the static and the night\r\nYou were my only guiding light\r\nMore than a feeling, more than a name\r\nYou were the fire, you were the flame\r\nThat led me through the pouring rain\r\nAnd brought me back to life again\r\n\r\n[Instrumental Outro]\r\n(A gentle, melodic, and deeply soulful saxophone solo plays, full of quiet reflection and gratitude. If the darkness comes back, she won't just call his name—she will physically **drag** him back to safety.\r\n\r\n**The Sonic Duel:**\r\nIn a unique production choice, the track features a duel between a heavy, distorted electric guitar (representing the threat/trauma) and a piercing, aggressive **Flute** (representing Luna).\r\n\r\nThe guitar tries to dominate the mix, but the flute weaves through the distortion, matching it note-for-note in intensity and volume.     "type": "track",
    "content": "**LORE NOTE:**\r\nA mature, reflective rock track that serves as the thematic resolution of the album.\r\n\r\nIf \"Sanctuary\" was the relief of rescue, **\"The Scar Remains\"** is the hard work of recovery. Leo acknowledges that he cannot simply \"reset\" to the boy he was in Track 1 (\"Nineteen and Invincible\"). That version of him is gone, lost to the \"Long Road West.\"\r\n\r\nInstead of fighting this loss, he accepts it. The lyrics describe the slow, non-linear process of learning to trust again—not just his family, but his own judgment.\r\n\r\n**The Sonic Synthesis:**\r\nMusically, this track represents the complete integration of the band's sound. It features **Luna's Flute** (representing her emotional promise) weaving seamlessly with **Stella's New Wave Synthesizer** (representing her structural protection), both grounded by a steady, classic 80s rock beat. It is the sound of his support system becoming part of his own internal rhythm.\r\n\r\n**LYRICS:**\r\n\r\n[Verse 1]\r\nI'm trying to find that boy I knew\r\nIn shades of brilliant white and blue\r\nThe one who thought he had it made\r\nBefore the colors start to fade\r\nHe's still in here, a distant ghost\r\nThe one I miss and fear the most\r\nA part of me is lost, it's true\r\nA space that was reserved for you... Olivia\r\n\r\n[Chorus]\r\nBut the scars remain, a map to trace\r\nAnd they have found their proper place\r\nI'm standing on a different floor\r\nThan I was standing on before\r\nAnd I can hear a brand new sound\r\nOn this new and solid ground\r\nThe echo of a worried flute\r\nA synthesizer's loving root\r\n\r\n[Verse 2]\r\nI'm learning how to trust again\r\nTo let the gentle sunlight in\r\nI'm learning that a helping hand\r\nCan help a broken boy to stand\r\nI'm learning that a home can be\r\nA fortress built for only me\r\nBy two strong women, brave and true\r\nWho know exactly what to do\r\n\r\n[Chorus]\r\n'Cause the scars remain, a map to trace\r\nAnd they have found their proper place\r\nI'm standing on a different floor\r\nThan I was standing on before\r\nAnd I can hear a brand new sound\r\nOn this new and solid ground\r\nThe echo of a worried flute\r\nA synthesizer's loving root\r\n\r\n[Instrumental Outro]\r\n(The flute and synthesizer trade melodies, harmonizing over the rock beat before fading into a warm, sustained chord.)"

She directly addresses the specific traumas Delores inflicted—the \"shifting sands\" of gaslighting, the \"slamming doors\" of isolation, and the \"cage\" of non-consensual touch.\r\n\r\nShe counters each trauma with a permanent architectural promise: **\"This House Stands.\"** It is a declaration that their family unit is a fortress that will never break. She swears that he will never face abuse again, not because the world is safe, but because *she* is standing between him and the world.\r\n\r\n**LYRICS:**\r\n\r\n[Verse 1]\r\nYou learned to live in shifting sands\r\nWith no safe place to ever land\r\nYou learned that doors could slam and lock\r\nAgainst the ticking of the clock\r\nYou learned that touch could be a cage\r\nA quiet, simmering, bitter rage\r\nBut that old world is dead and gone\r\nIt's time to face a brand new dawn\r\n\r\n[Chorus]\r\nSo let my arms be your new walls\r\nTo catch you every time you fall\r\nThis house stands, it will not break\r\nFor your own precious, gentle sake\r\nIf it's a hug that you should need\r\nA simple, pure, and loving creed\r\nThen I am here, my love is true\r\nMy arms will be a home for you\r\n\r\n[Verse 2]\r\nNo need to brace, no need to hide\r\nWith your two sisters by your side\r\nWe'll be the lock, we'll be the key\r\nTo your own safe reality\r\nWe'll be the roof against the rain\r\nTo wash away the hurt and pain\r\nThis is a vow we'll always keep\r\nWhile you are in your troubled sleep\r\n\r\n[Guitar Solo]\r\n(A clean, melodic, and triumphant guitar solo. The 13-track album was too long for a standard vinyl LP, so Apex executives \"gleefully\" cut this short, 1:35 track from the original vinyl release to save space. They viewed it as \"noise,\" but to Ryan, cutting his rawest song was a direct attempt to silence the \"Engine.\"\r\n\r\n**LYRICS:**\r\n\r\n(Verse 1)\r\nTick-tock goes the plastic clock\r\nAnother meeting on another block\r\nThey're selling me a future that I don't want\r\nIt's just the same old tired font\r\n\r\n(Chorus)\r\nI'm out of patience!\r\nI'm out of time!\r\nThis ain't your product, this life is mine!\r\nOut of patience!\r\nOut of time!\r\nGonna break free, gonna cross the line!\r\n\r\n(Verse 2)\r\nYou want another ballad, soft and slow\r\nAnother song for the radio\r\nBut I've got a fire burning in my hands\r\nYou'll never, ever understand\r\n\r\n(Chorus)\r\nI'm out of patience!\r\nI'm out of time!\r\nThis ain't your product, this life is mine!\r\nOut of patience!\r\nOut of time!\r\nGonna break free, gonna cross the line!\r\n\r\n(Guitar Solo)\r\n(Short, fast, and chaotic. Pure punk-rock energy)\r\n\r\n(Chorus)\r\nI'm out of patience!\r\nI'm out of time!\r\nThis ain't your product, this life is mine!\r\nOut of patience!\r\nOut of time!\r\nGonna break free, gonna cross the line!\r\n\r\n(Outro)\r\nOut of time!\r\n(Song ends abruptly on a final, crashing chord)"

As \"punishment\" for the heavy rock tracks earlier on Side B, Apex ordered him to close the album with a quiet ballad, explicitly forbidding \"pounding drums\" on the track sheet. [cite_start]Ryan complied by recording the song using **percussive acoustic guitar**—slapping the body of the instrument to simulate a beat[cite: 353]. It was a subtle, rhythmic \"middle finger\" to the executives who tried to silence the \"Engine.\"\r\n\r\n**LYRICS:**\r\n\r\n(Verse 1)\r\nThe stage is empty, the crowd is gone\r\nAnother night, another dawn\r\nWe played the part, we sang the song\r\nAnd now we have to move along\r\n\r\n(Verse 2)\r\nThe neon heart is turning low\r\nIt's just a faded, distant glow\r\nIs this the dream we wanted so?\r\nI guess we'll never know\r\n\r\n(Chorus)\r\nWe're just echoes in the city\r\nIt's not so bad, it's not so pretty\r\nJust echoes, turning to gray\r\nWe'll come back and fight another day\r\n\r\n(Bridge)\r\n(A soft, melodic acoustic guitar solo, underscored by the rhythmic slapping of the guitar body)\r\nAnother day...\r\nAnother way...\r\n\r\n(Chorus)\r\nWe're just echoes in the city\r\nIt's not so bad, it's not so pretty\r\nJust echoes, turning to gray\r\nWe'll come back and fight another day\r\n\r\n(Outro)\r\nAfter the show...\r\nGoodnight...\r\n(fades out on acoustic guitar)"

In the intro, Ryan O'Connell—who, like his sister, is autistic and values absolute clarity—explicitly introduces Cassidy as \"my sister,\" publicly disproving the \"married couple\" assumption years before it destroyed their contract.\r\n\r\nMusically, this recording is a source of fury for the band. It was the only concession Apex made—allowing them to play their self-titled anthem as the opener of their strict 30-minute support slot. However, the mix is \"sabotaged\": Cassidy's iconic \"Stardust\" synth leitmotif—the musical heart of the song—is buried so deep in the mix it is practically inaudible, leaving only the \"Engine\" rock elements.\r\n\r\n**LYRICS:**\r\n\r\n(Spoken Intro)\r\nHello Chicago!     "content": "**LORE NOTE:**\r\nFor Cassidy, who is autistic, the lyrics of this song were not a performance—they were a terrifying \"what-if\" scenario that she experienced viscerally every time she sang it. While the studio marketed it as a romantic breakup ballad, Cassidy sang it as a desperate plea to her brother. **The fear in her voice on this recording is real.**\r\n\r\nRyan, who is also autistic, understood her need for absolute certainty better than anyone. He famously had to reassure her after *every single concert*—often using the exact same phrase, **\"Family sticks together\"**—to ground her back in reality and confirm that he was never going to leave her.\r\n\r\n**Note: After this specific performance, Cassidy broke down in tears backstage. Ryan had to hold her in a deep pressure hug for twenty minutes to help her reset and remind her that he was right there.**\r\n\r\n**LYRICS:**\r\n\r\n(Spoken Intro)\r\nThank you.     "id": "1-01-escape-velocity-ad-astra",
    "title": "Escape Velocity (Ad Astra) [Full Suite]",
    "artist": "The Stardust Engine",

    "type": "track",
    "content": "**LORE NOTE:**\r\nThe \"Magnum Opus.\" Originally released as the B-Side of *The Warehouse Tapes*, this suite stands here as the monumental A-Side it was always meant to be.\r\n\r\nThis 15-minute progressive rock journey is Cassidy O'Connell's autobiography in four movements, charting her escape from the trauma of the 'Friction' trial (\"The Cage\") to the transcendence of total artistic freedom (\"The Cosmos\").\r\n\r\nKnown by fans simply as **\"Ad Astra,\"** the track features a distinct \"mechanical cutoff\" sound between Part II and Part III. This wasn't a studio effect; Ryan O'Connell physically pulled the plug on his guitar amplifier mid-recording to simulate the violent silence of a rocket's Main Engine Cutoff (MECO).\r\n\r\nIn the band's \"Ironworks\" canon, this track acts as a coded transmission broadcast from Port Telsus, sending safe navigational coordinates to resistance fighters hidden in The Weave.\r\n\r\n**LYRICS:**\r\n\r\n[Part I: The Cage]\r\n(Whispered, over a dark, shimmering synth pad)\r\nThe walls are cold, the air is thin\r\nThe noise outside is closing in\r\nA single light, a buzzing sound\r\nAnother day in the underground\r\n\r\nI'm running from the face I see\r\nThe \"victim-saint\" they made of me\r\nThe \"wholesome girl,\" so sad and worn\r\nA \"David\" for their Goliath's scorn\r\n\r\nThis rage is just a heavy stone\r\nA gravity I've outgrown\r\nShedding skin, shedding weight\r\nEscaping from this world of hate\r\n\r\nThey loved the trial, the \"family\" torn\r\nThey loved the story, a new star born\r\nBut not from music, not from grace\r\nFrom the \"hot-chocolate-drinking\" \"angel face\"\r\n\r\nThe \"Shark\" was right, the \"nuke\" went off\r\nWe won the battle, but at what cost?\r\nWe're \"radioactive,\" we're \"litigious\"\r\nWe're just the joke, the \"victim-saints\"\r\n\r\n(Slightly louder)\r\nI'm trapped inside this \"Wilderness\"\r\nThis \"Fortress\" built on bitterness\r\nThis isn't freedom, this is a cell\r\nThis \"righteous hell\"...\r\nThis \"righteous hell\"...\r\n\r\n[Part II: Ignition (Ad Astra)]\r\n(The dark synth pad is suddenly cut by a rapid, driving analog synth sequencer. A pounding, 80s acoustic drum beat kicks in, locked with a driving bassline. Vocals become strong, clear, and melodic.)\r\n\r\nI see a signal in the endless black\r\nA single light, no turning back\r\nThe pull of the old world fades away\r\nA new horizon, a new day\r\n\r\nThe countdown's running in my head\r\nIgnoring all the things they said\r\nThe \"Engine\" roars, a crimson light\r\nA pillar of fire in the night\r\n\r\nThe pressure builds, I'm pinned to the seat\r\nMy heart is pounding with the beat\r\nThe G-force pulling, a heavy stone\r\nThe last chain breaks, I'm not alone\r\n\r\n(Chorus)\r\nI'm climbing! Climbing!\r\nPast the point of no return\r\nI'm flying! Flying!\r\nWith a fire I let burn\r\nJust a signal, a distant light\r\nBurning through the endless night\r\nAd astra... (To the stars!)\r\nAd astra!\r\n\r\n(Instrumental Break)\r\n(A short, fast, synthesizer and guitar riff-heavy section. Pure 80s prog-rock energy.)\r\n\r\nThe sky is tearing at the seams\r\nAwake now from their broken dreams\r\nThe \"Stardust\" is just a trail of dust\r\nThe \"Stardust\" is the thing I trust\r\n\r\nThe clouds are falling far below\r\nA place of pain I used to know\r\nThe blue gives way to endless black\r\nThere is no thought of turning back\r\n\r\n(Chorus)\r\nI'm climbing! Climbing!\r\nPast the point of no return\r\nI'm flying! Flying!\r\nWith a fire I let burn\r\nJust a signal, a distant light\r\nBurning through the endless night\r\nAd astra... (To the stars!)\r\nAd astra!\r\n\r\n[Part III: The Cosmos]\r\n(The driving beat cuts off abruptly. Tempo slows to a crawl. Vast, sweeping synth pads take over.)\r\n\r\nI'm floating now, the engine's off\r\nThe silence is a holy sound\r\nThe rage is gone, the fear is lost\r\nIn this new universe I've found\r\n\r\nThe \"Cage\" is just a distant star\r\nA tiny light I left behind\r\nI finally know just who you are\r\nThe other half of my own mind\r\n\r\n(Ethereal, layered vocals swell)\r\nI'm loved by the sun\r\nI'm held by the void\r\nAn orbit just begun\r\nA life not destroyed\r\n\r\nI'm loved by the moon\r\nI'm cradled by the black\r\nMy heart is back in tune\r\nThere is no turning back\r\n\r\nAnd the stars are singing\r\nA song I understand\r\nI'm finally, finally\r\nReaching for your hand\r\n\r\n(Instrumental Bridge)\r\n(Music swells. A long, beautiful, melodic, and highly emotional electric guitar solo. Full of hope, not rage.)\r\n\r\n(Ethereal, layered vocals return)\r\nI see the colors of the nebula\r\nA place I've only seen in dreams\r\nThe reds, the blues, the purples call\r\nFlowing in majestic streams\r\n\r\nThis is the place I was meant to be\r\nThis is the \"Stardust\" that is me\r\nThe cosmos and the rocket's fire\r\nMy one, my only true desire\r\n\r\n(Ethereal, layered vocals)\r\nI'm loved by the sun\r\nI'm held by the void\r\nAn orbit just begun\r\nA life not destroyed\r\n\r\nAnd the stars are singing\r\nA song I understand\r\nI'm finally, finally\r\nReaching for your hand\r\n\r\n[Part IV: Re-entry (The New World)]\r\n(Music slowly fades back to just the shimmering synth pads and ethereal, whispered vocals)\r\n\r\nThe walls are gone\r\nThe air is clear\r\nThere is no noise\r\nThere is no fear\r\n\r\nThe \"Stardust\" is a forgotten ghost\r\nThe \"Engine\" has delivered me to the coast\r\nOf a new world, a new shore\r\nA place I've never been before\r\n\r\nThe \"Cage\" is broken, the \"Victim-Saint\" is dead\r\nA new, clean script is in my head\r\nThe \"Friction\" burned away in the fire\r\nLeaving only my true desire\r\n\r\n(Ethereal, layered vocals swell for the finale)\r\nI'm loved by the sun...\r\nI'm held by the void...\r\nMy orbit has begun...\r\nMy life is not destroyed...\r\n\r\nAd astra...\r\nAd astra...\r\n(To the stars...)\r\n\r\n(Song fades out on a final, sustained, peaceful synthesizer chord)"

    "type": "track",
    "content": "**LORE NOTE:**\r\nThe \"Trojan Horse\" edit. Knowing that mainstream radio would never play a 15-minute prog-rock suite, the band (at Holly's insistence) cut this condensed 5-minute version for the promo CD.\r\n\r\nIt strips away the extended ambient transitions and long instrumental solos, focusing purely on the core narrative arc from \"The Cage\" to \"Re-entry.\" While die-hard \"Ironheads\" consider it incomplete, this is the version that actually charted, introducing millions of new fans to the band's \"Freedom Era\" sound.\r\n\r\n**LYRICS:**\r\n\r\n[Part I: The Cage]\r\n(Whispered, over a dark, shimmering synth pad)\r\nThe walls are cold, the air is thin\r\nThe noise outside is closing in\r\nI'm running from the face I see\r\nThe \"victim-saint\" they made of me\r\nThis rage is just a heavy stone\r\nA gravity I've outgrown\r\nShedding skin, shedding weight\r\nEscaping from this world of hate\r\n\r\n[Part II: Ignition (Ad Astra)]\r\n(A driving, 80s analog synth sequencer starts, bass and drums kick in)\r\n(Vocals become strong and melodic)\r\nI see a light in the endless black\r\nA signal calling, no turning back\r\nThe pull of the old world fades away\r\nA new horizon, a new day\r\n\r\n(Chorus)\r\n(Full band, soaring ethereal vocals)\r\nI'm climbing, climbing\r\nPast the point of no return\r\nI'm flying, flying\r\nWith a fire I let burn\r\nJust a signal, a distant light\r\nBurning through the endless night\r\nAd astra... (To the stars!)\r\n\r\n[Part III: The Cosmos (Loved by the Sun)]\r\n(Music becomes vast, atmospheric, and beautiful. Grand, sweeping synth pads and melodic guitar)\r\nI'm floating now, the engine's off\r\nThe silence is a holy sound\r\nThe rage is gone, the fear is lost\r\nIn this new universe I've found\r\n\r\nI'm loved by the sun\r\nI'm held by the void\r\nAn orbit just begun\r\nA life not destroyed\r\nAnd the stars are singing\r\nA song I understand\r\nI'm finally, finally\r\nReaching for your hand\r\n\r\n(Instrumental Bridge)\r\n(A long, melodic, soaring electric guitar solo, full of emotion and hope)\r\n\r\n[Part IV: Re-entry (The New World)]\r\n(Music slowly fades back to just the shimmering synth pads and ethereal, whispered vocals)\r\nThe walls are gone\r\nThe air is clear\r\nThere is no noise\r\nThere is no fear\r\nAd astra...\r\nAd astra...\r\n(fades out)"

The band wrote this song about the literal, scientific friction of a space shuttle re-entering Earth's atmosphere (\"Here comes the friction / Feel the fire\"). The \"rogue\" executive, Julian Vance, misinterpreted these lyrics as sexual metaphors, leading to the catastrophic misunderstanding that destroyed the contract. This is the original 1992 demo, released here for the first time.\r\n\r\n**LYRICS:**\r\n\r\n(Verse 1)\r\nBlack velvet, a million lights\r\nFloating silent through the endless night\r\nThe world below, a sleeping blue\r\nI'm holding my breath, I'm thinking of you\r\n\r\n(Verse 2)\r\nThe signal's given, the turn is made\r\nThe point of no return, the price is paid\r\nThe fragile hull begins to shake\r\nA promise that we're bound to make\r\n\r\n(Chorus)\r\nHere comes the friction, feel the fire\r\nBurning up with our desire\r\nA sheet of flame, a crimson glow\r\nThe only way we know to go\r\nWe're coming home, we're breaking through\r\nThis atmosphere... with you\r\n\r\n(Verse 3)\r\nThe pressure builds, the gauges scream\r\nThis is the violence of the dream\r\nA single tear in the metal skin\r\nIs all it takes to let the outside in\r\n\r\n(Chorus)\r\nHere comes the friction, feel the fire\r\nBurning up with our desire\r\nA sheet of flame, a crimson glow\r\nThe only way we know to go\r\nWe're coming home, we're breaking through\r\nThis atmosphere... with you\r\n\r\n(Bridge)\r\nWe're losing control, the edges blur\r\nA terror and a beauty...\r\nBurning up!\r\n\r\n(Guitar Solo)\r\n\r\n(Chorus)\r\nHere comes the friction, feel the fire\r\nBurning up with our desire\r\nA sheet of flame, a crimson glow\r\nThe only way we know to go\r\nWe're coming home, we're breaking through\r\nThis atmosphere... with you\r\n\r\n(Outro)\r\nBurning up...\r\nFriction...\r\nComing home...\r\n(fades)"

    "type": "track",
    "content": "**LORE NOTE:**\r\nRyan's raw, \"jangle-rock\" rage track. It retells the infamous \"dirty mirror\" incident from the 1992 *Friction* photo shoot, where an executive tried to offer him cocaine on a mirror, and Ryan—innocently thinking it was dirt—wiped it clean with a napkin. The song channels his fury at the executive who humiliated him and tried to destroy his family.\r\n\r\n**LYRICS:**\r\n\r\n(Verse 1)\r\nYou brought your tray, your silver plate\r\nYou thought you'd open up the gate\r\nYou showed me powder on a glass\r\nYou thought you had a man of class\r\n\r\n(Chorus)\r\nIt was just dust! It was just a lie!\r\nSo I wiped it clean, and I watched you die\r\nYou called it 'art', you called it 'style'\r\nIt was just garbage in a filthy pile!\r\n\r\n(Verse 2)\r\nYou held your clipboard like a gun\r\nYou thought the battle was already won\r\nYou crossed the line, you showed your hand\r\nYou'll never, ever understand\r\n\r\n(Chorus)\r\nIt was just dust! It was just a lie!\r\nSo I wiped it clean, and I watched you die\r\nYou called it 'art', you called it 'style'\r\nIt was just garbage in a filthy pile!\r\n\r\n(Guitar Solo)\r\n\r\n(Chorus)\r\nIt was just dust! It was just a lie!\r\nSo I wiped it clean, and I watched you die\r\nYou called it 'art', 'cuz you're a fake\r\nIt was just garbage, make no mistake!"

    "id": "1-06-escape-velocity-ad-astra",
    "title": "Escape Velocity (Ad Astra)",
    "artist": "The Stardust Engine",
    "album": "The Warehouse Tapes",
    "url": "C:/Program Files/Git/engine-room/artists/the-stardust-engine/albums/1995-the-warehouse-tapes",
    "type": "track",
    "content": "**LORE NOTE:**\r\nThe \"Magnum Opus\" that consumes the entirety of Side B. This 15-minute progressive rock suite is Cassidy O'Connell's autobiographical journey from the trauma of the trial (\"The Cage\") to the transcendence of artistic freedom (\"The Cosmos\").\r\n\r\nKnown by fans simply as **\"Ad Astra,\"** the track features a distinct \"mechanical cutoff\" sound between Part II and Part III, created by Ryan pulling the plug on his guitar amp to simulate a rocket's Main Engine Cutoff (MECO).\r\n\r\n**LYRICS:**\r\n\r\n[Part I: The Cage]\r\n(Whispered, over a dark, shimmering synth pad)\r\nThe walls are cold, the air is thin\r\nThe noise outside is closing in\r\nA single light, a buzzing sound\r\nAnother day in the underground\r\n\r\nI'm running from the face I see\r\nThe \"victim-saint\" they made of me\r\nThe \"wholesome girl,\" so sad and worn\r\nA \"David\" for their Goliath's scorn\r\n\r\nThis rage is just a heavy stone\r\nA gravity I've outgrown\r\nShedding skin, shedding weight\r\nEscaping from this world of hate\r\n\r\nThey loved the trial, the \"family\" torn\r\nThey loved the story, a new star born\r\nBut not from music, not from grace\r\nFrom the \"hot-chocolate-drinking\" \"angel face\"\r\n\r\nThe \"Shark\" was right, the \"nuke\" went off\r\nWe won the battle, but at what cost?\r\nWe're \"radioactive,\" we're \"litigious\"\r\nWe're just the joke, the \"victim-saints\"\r\n\r\n(Slightly louder)\r\nI'm trapped inside this \"Wilderness\"\r\nThis \"Fortress\" built on bitterness\r\nThis isn't freedom, this is a cell\r\nThis \"righteous hell\"...\r\nThis \"righteous hell\"...\r\n\r\n[Part II: Ignition (Ad Astra)]\r\n(The dark synth pad is suddenly cut by a rapid, driving analog synth sequencer. A pounding, 80s acoustic drum beat kicks in, locked with a driving bassline. Vocals become strong, clear, and melodic.)\r\n\r\nI see a signal in the endless black\r\nA single light, no turning back\r\nThe pull of the old world fades away\r\nA new horizon, a new day\r\n\r\nThe countdown's running in my head\r\nIgnoring all the things they said\r\nThe \"Engine\" roars, a crimson light\r\nA pillar of fire in the night\r\n\r\nThe pressure builds, I'm pinned to the seat\r\nMy heart is pounding with the beat\r\nThe G-force pulling, a heavy stone\r\nThe last chain breaks, I'm not alone\r\n\r\n(Chorus)\r\nI'm climbing! Climbing!\r\nPast the point of no return\r\nI'm flying! Flying!\r\nWith a fire I let burn\r\nJust a signal, a distant light\r\nBurning through the endless night\r\nAd astra... (To the stars!)\r\nAd astra!\r\n\r\n(Instrumental Break)\r\n(A short, fast, synthesizer and guitar riff-heavy section. Pure 80s prog-rock energy.)\r\n\r\nThe sky is tearing at the seams\r\nAwake now from their broken dreams\r\nThe \"Stardust\" is just a trail of dust\r\nThe \"Stardust\" is the thing I trust\r\n\r\nThe clouds are falling far below\r\nA place of pain I used to know\r\nThe blue gives way to endless black\r\nThere is no thought of turning back\r\n\r\n(Chorus)\r\nI'm climbing! Climbing!\r\nPast the point of no return\r\nI'm flying! Flying!\r\nWith a fire I let burn\r\nJust a signal, a distant light\r\nBurning through the endless night\r\nAd astra... (To the stars!)\r\nAd astra!\r\n\r\n[Part III: The Cosmos]\r\n(The driving beat cuts off abruptly. Tempo slows to a crawl. Vast, sweeping synth pads take over.)\r\n\r\nI'm floating now, the engine's off\r\nThe silence is a holy sound\r\nThe rage is gone, the fear is lost\r\nIn this new universe I've found\r\n\r\nThe \"Cage\" is just a distant star\r\nA tiny light I left behind\r\nI finally know just who you are\r\nThe other half of my own mind\r\n\r\n(Ethereal, layered vocals swell)\r\nI'm loved by the sun\r\nI'm held by the void\r\nAn orbit just begun\r\nA life not destroyed\r\n\r\nI'm loved by the moon\r\nI'm cradled by the black\r\nMy heart is back in tune\r\nThere is no turning back\r\n\r\nAnd the stars are singing\r\nA song I understand\r\nI'm finally, finally\r\nReaching for your hand\r\n\r\n(Instrumental Bridge)\r\n(Music swells. A long, beautiful, melodic, and highly emotional electric guitar solo. Full of hope, not rage.)\r\n\r\n(Ethereal, layered vocals return)\r\nI see the colors of the nebula\r\nA place I've only seen in dreams\r\nThe reds, the blues, the purples call\r\nFlowing in majestic streams\r\n\r\nThis is the place I was meant to be\r\nThis is the \"Stardust\" that is me\r\nThe cosmos and the rocket's fire\r\nMy one, my only true desire \r\nI'm loved by the sun\r\n\r\n(Ethereal, layered vocalization)\r\n\r\n[Part IV: Re-entry (The New World)]\r\n(Music slowly fades back to just the shimmering synth pads and ethereal, whispered vocals)\r\n\r\nThe walls are gone\r\nThe air is clear\r\nThere is no noise\r\nThere is no fear\r\n\r\nThe \"Stardust\" is a forgotten ghost\r\nThe \"Engine\" has delivered me to the coast\r\nOf a new world, a new shore\r\nA place I've never been before\r\n\r\nThe \"Cage\" is broken, the \"Victim-Saint\" is dead\r\nA new, clean script is in my head\r\nThe \"Friction\" burned away in the fire\r\nLeaving only my true desire\r\n\r\n(Ethereal, layered vocals swell for the finale)\r\nI'm loved by the sun...\r\nI'm held by the void...\r\nMy orbit has begun...\r\nMy life is not destroyed...\r\n\r\nAd astra...\r\nAd astra...\r\n(To the stars...)\r\n\r\n(Song fades out on a final, sustained, peaceful synthesizer chord)"

--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/1-01-nineteen-and-invincible.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics
// FILE:      1-01-nineteen-and-invincible.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/1-01-nineteen-and-invincible.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   11
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
The album's powerful "before" picture. This two-part anthem opens with a heartfelt 80s power ballad capturing Leo's profound sense of safety and contentment with his "three guiding stars"—his sisters, Stella and Luna, and his best friend, Olivia.

The song then explodes into a triumphant rock track, representing his boundless, naive optimism for the future. This is the sound of his complete, happy, and authentic self—the world that is about to be shattered by the events to come.

**LYRICS:**

[Verse 1]
The college books are open wide
With my whole world right by my side
A quiet laugh, a knowing look
A chapter from a favorite book
The future's just a distant shore
I couldn't ask for anything more
Than this small room, this gentle space
A perfect time, a perfect place

[Chorus]
And I've got my three guiding stars
Healing all my childhood scars
A sister's strength, a sister's grace
A best friend's warm and kind embrace
This is the only life I need
A perfect, rare, and precious creed
Yeah, I'm nineteen and invincible
With a love that's unconditional

[Verse 2]
We talk for hours on the phone
I never feel like I'm alone
You get the jokes inside my head
On every single word unsaid
We're just a team of misfit souls
Making each other brave and whole
No need to search, no need to roam
'Cause with the three of you, I'm home

[Intro]
(A powerful, triumphant 80s rock drum fill kicks in, and the song explodes with driving electric guitars and a soaring synthesizer melody.)

[Verse 3]
Yeah, the world is out there, big and wide
But I've got nothing left to hide
With my team right here, my crew, my friends
The story never has to end
We're gonna chase the summer sun
Our real adventure's just begun
Got a full tank and a radio
And everywhere is ours to go!

[Chorus]
And I feel the rhythm, I feel the beat
Of my own heart on a crowded street
We're coming back to the kids we knew
In shades of brilliant white and blue
This is the sound of being free
This is the sound of being me!

[Guitar Solo]
(A soaring, melodic, and triumphant 80s rock guitar solo, full of emotion and a sense of release.)

[Chorus]
And I feel the rhythm, I feel the beat
Of my own heart on a crowded street
We're coming back to the kids we knew
In shades of brilliant white and blue
This is the sound of being free
This is the sound of being me!


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/1-02-a-new-light.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics
// FILE:      1-02-a-new-light.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/1-02-a-new-light.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   17
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
An upbeat 80s rock anthem capturing Leo's naive excitement at meeting Delores (Vanessa). He is still reeling from his best friend Olivia moving away, and Delores feels like a hopeful new beginning.

However, a **prominent, worried flute melody** plays throughout the entire track, a stark contrast to the song's otherwise optimistic tone. This is Luna's "Playing the Flute" signal—her desperate, unheard warning to her brother that this new light is casting a very dark shadow.

**LYRICS:**

[Verse 1]
The world felt gray, a missing piece
A quiet, slow, and sad release
A chapter closed, a best friend gone
Just trying to find a way to carry on
The days were long, the colors faded
A little lost and worn and jaded
Then you walked in and caught my eye
Beneath a dull community college sky

[Verse 2]
You talk of dreams and taking flight
Of leaving all the dark for light
You say you see the real me there
A kindred soul beyond compare
It feels so fast, it feels so right
A sudden star in my dark night
You fill the void, you bridge the gap
A perfect escape

[Chorus: Female Singer]
I see a new light in your lonely room
But there's a shadow chasing after you
You see a fast car on an open road
I see a heavy, heavy load
And for the first time in a long, long while...

[Chorus: Male Singer]
And you're a new light in a lonely room
Chasing away the coming gloom
You're a fast car on an open road
Lifting off a heavy load
And for the first time in a long, long while...

[Outro: Female Singer]
(Sung from Luna's perspective)
A new light...
Oh, my dear brother, please be smart
This new light's going to break your heart...
It's going to break your heart...


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/1-03-painted-smile.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics
// FILE:      1-03-painted-smile.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/1-03-painted-smile.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   23
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
A multi-part epic that musically depicts Leo's primary survival mechanism: the **"Painted Smile."**

**1. The Mask:**
The "Painted Smile" is defined as a "hollow mask of endurance." It is not a genuine expression of happiness; it is a rigid, forced facade of compliance that Leo locks into place to hide his fear from Delores. It serves as a survival shield, protecting his "broken parts" from a predator who feeds on his emotional reactions.

**2. The Sonic Shift:**
The song begins as a gentle 80s power ballad, representing his authentic, safe self when he is with his sisters. As Delores's presence looms over the "Sibling Weekend," the track transitions into a tense, driving rock bridge. Finally, in the closing moments, the music violently shatters into a **cold, heavy industrial sound**. This sonic shift mimics the dissociation Leo experiences—becoming mechanical and silent to endure the trauma.

**3. The Narrative Arc:**
This track marks the moment the trap snaps shut. It establishes the psychological barrier Leo will live behind for the entirety of Disc 1. The mask will not fall until the track "Sanctuary" on Disc 2, when he is finally safe on his native ground.

**LYRICS:**

[Verse 1]
Just one more day, just one more night

An anxious, hypnotic instrumental that marks the beginning of the four-day bus trip from **Norfolk, Virginia**.

The music covers the initial, exhausting leg of the journey: the bus change in Richmond, the long haul through Charlottesville and Roanoke, and the crossing into Tennessee via Bristol and Knoxville.

The monotonous, driving beat and repetitive synth-bass mimic the endless highway and the vibration of the bus floor. This is the sound of the journey before Leo is consciously aware of the danger; he feels that something is fundamentally wrong with this "adventure," but he can't yet articulate the fear that is slowly building with every mile he travels further from home.

But **Delores** convinces him there is "no time." She weaponizes his fear of being a burden, silencing him before he can advocate for himself, and rushes him onto the next coach like luggage.

--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/1-15-a-night-in-kent.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics
// FILE:      1-15-a-night-in-kent.md

// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   95
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
A dark, claustrophobic, and beatless industrial ambient track set in **Kent, Washington**.

The title comes from Delores's aunt, Carol Mahone, who cheerfully welcomes them to her home, calling it "A Slice of Heaven." To Leo, trapped in the guest room with no way to communicate his location to the outside world, it is "A Slice of Hell."

The music represents the oppressive, suffocating silence of the house. However, buried deep in the mix is a subtle, warmer texture—a shift in the drone. This represents **Carol's quiet observation**. Unlike Delores, she notices Leo's physical pain (the pressure sores) and his fear. She silently sides with him, sensing that her niece's story doesn't add up, though she does not yet speak out.

**LYRICS:**

[Instrumental]

(The track opens with no drum beat—only a low, oppressive, industrial drone that hums and pulses, representing the stuffy heat of the unwelcoming house.)

(A detuned, slightly off-key piano plays a sparse, repeating, and unsettling melody. It sounds like a warped lullaby.)

(The drone grows louder and more dissonant, creating a sense of intense physical discomfort and dread—the tense silence before the violent outburst that will happen the next morning.)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/1-16-shattered-voice-silent-explosion.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics
// FILE:      1-16-shattered-voice-silent-explosion.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/1-16-shattered-voice-silent-explosion.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   101
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
A short, violent, and jarring industrial instrumental that serves as the album's harrowing climax and the end of Disc 1.

It depicts the final confrontation in the guest room in **Kent, Washington**. Delores, enraged by Leo's silent request to go home (typed on his device), screams at him and physically rips the AAC machine from his hands. The track builds to a single, massive, distorted metallic **CRASH**—the sound of the device hitting the linoleum floor and shattering.

This is the "Death of the Voice." The crash is followed by a long, dead silence, the sound of freezing rain, and finally, the cold, mechanical click of the front door locking him out.

**Production Note:** The "Crash" sound effect was created by Julian Croft recording the sound of a vintage 1999 Quantum Communicator being dropped from a ladder in the studio.

**LYRICS:**

[Instrumental]

(The track begins with a chaotic, fast, and distorted drum machine loop at 160 BPM, overlaid with a frantic, dissonant synthesizer arpeggio that mimics the cadence of a screaming argument.)

(The noise builds to an unbearable crescendo, layering distortion upon distortion until—)

**[CRASH]**

(A single, massive, ear-splitting metallic impact. The music cuts out instantly.)

(Two seconds of absolute, dead silence.)

(The sound of cold, heavy rain begins to fade in, accompanied by a single, high-pitched, humming synthesizer note representing the ringing in Leo's ears.)

(The track ends with the heavy, final sound of a door slamming and the *click* of a deadbolt sliding home.)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/2-01-curbstone-4-am.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics
// FILE:      2-01-curbstone-4-am.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/2-01-curbstone-4-am.md

// TRACK NO:   107
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
The centerpiece of the album and the start of **Disc 2: The Journey Home**.

This is Leo's raw, unfiltered internal monologue immediately after Delores locks him out of the house in **Kent, Washington**. He is sitting in his wheelchair on a wet concrete curb at 4:00 AM. The rain is driving, soaking through his favorite navy blue sweatshirt, his jeans, his shoes, and his socks.

He is physically shattered—shivering violently, suffering from medication withdrawal cramps, and **starving**. His backpack is strapped to the back of his chair, containing the shards of his destroyed AAC device. He has no voice and no plan, except for a couple of crumpled dollar bills in his wallet—money meant for vending machines that is now his only hope for fare on the **TAPS** (Transit Authority of the Puget Sound) bus to the airport.

**The "Rootkit Key":** This song captures the trauma so perfectly that it becomes a permanent, override-proof trigger for Leo. In later years, hearing just the opening notes will bypass his logic centers and cause an immediate autistic meltdown. This reaction is absolute: even if he is **actively being grounded** by all three women (Luna, Olivia, and Stella) and even if they **warn him** the song is about to play, the trigger cannot be stopped.

**LYRICS:**

[Intro]
(Starts with the sound of cold, pouring rain and a deep, pulsing synthesizer bass. A heavy, distorted drum machine beat kicks in—slow and menacing.)

[Verse 1]
The front door clicks, a final sound
A world of silence all around
Just pouring rain on cold blacktop
A spinning life that has to stop
My voice of shattered, useless glass
Is packed away, a future past
This curbstone is an icy throne
I've never felt so all alone

[Chorus]
Just gotta get to the airport
Don't care how, don't care what it costs
A thousand miles from my safe port
A lifeline I can't bear to have lost
This city's just a concrete cage
Gotta turn another page
Just gotta get to the airport
It's the only prayer I've got

[Verse 2]
The streetlights bleed a hazy glare
On empty houses, standing there
My arms are burning, wheels are slow
A dozen blocks still left to go
The chill gets down inside my bones
To whispered fears and silent moans
Is anybody watching me?
Or am I just too lost to see?

[Chorus]
Just gotta get to the airport
Don't care how, don't care what it costs
A thousand miles from my safe port
A lifeline I can't bear to have lost
This city's just a concrete cage
Gotta turn another page
Just gotta get to the airport
It's the only prayer I've got

[Bridge]
A flash of faces in my mind
The only true north I can find
A sister's love, a steady hand
Across this cold and broken land
It's just enough to keep me whole
To fight and reclaim my own soul...

[Guitar Solo]
(Not melodic or soaring. A raw, angry, and distorted guitar solo that sounds like a desperate cry, full of feedback and anguish.)

[Outro]
(Music fades slightly, leaving the drum machine, bass, and the sound of rain. Vocals become a desperate, repeated whisper.)
Airport... airport...
Gotta get there...
(A final, sharp synth stab and the sound of rain fades to silence.)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/2-02-165-to-burien.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics
// FILE:      2-02-165-to-burien.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/2-02-165-to-burien.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   113
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
A tense but driving instrumental that bridges the gap between the "Curbstone" and the "Airport."

This track covers the logistical nightmare of Leo's escape: navigating the **TAPS** (Transit Authority of the Puget Sound) bus network with no voice, no map, and a shattered communication device.
* **Leg 1:** The **165** from the Kent neighborhood to the Kent Train Station. A terrifying moment of vulnerability where Leo has to communicate his needs to strangers without words.
* **The Transfer:** The kindness of strangers: the Route 164 bus operator explained Leo's situation to their dispatcher over the radio, who then radioed ahead to the Route 161 bus to have them be prepared for Leo's arrival.
* **Leg 2:** The **161** from the station to **SEA-TAC Airport**.

The core emotional beat of this track is the **kindness of strangers**. Unlike Delores, the bus operators don't scream at him. They see a young man in a wheelchair, soaking wet, holding a broken screen, and terrified. They take the time to read his desperate, hand-signed gestures. They secure his chair. They get him to the right stop. It is the first humanity he has experienced in weeks.

**LYRICS:**

[Instrumental]

(The track opens with the pneumatic hiss of bus doors opening—a sound that used to signal entrapment, but now signals escape.)

(A steady, forward-moving bassline kicks in at 110 BPM. It is anxious but determined, mimicking the motion of the bus through the rainy morning streets.)

(The melody is carried by a clean, delayed electric guitar. It starts hesitant and shaky—representing Leo's fear of being rejected by the driver—but grows stronger and more melodic as the operator smiles and waves him onboard.)

(The bridge features a "mechanical shift" sound effect, representing the transfer at Kent Station. The tempo picks up slightly. The anxiety of the "High Desert" drone is gone, replaced by a driving, rhythmic focus. He is moving toward the planes.)

(The track ends with the distinct sound of an airport announcement chime.)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/2-03-a-tty-lifeline.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics
// FILE:      2-03-a-tty-lifeline.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/2-03-a-tty-lifeline.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   119
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
A tense, minimalist instrumental track built around the unique sonic texture of **Baudot tones** (the screeching data sounds of a TTY machine).

Leo has made it to **SEA-TAC Airport**, but his journey isn't over; he has no ticket and no money. He finds a TTY payphone bank. Having only ever used modern AAC devices, the archaic teletypewriter is alien to him. He doesn't know the protocols—"GA" (Go Ahead) or "SK" (Stop Keying).

Desperate, he dials `0` and types a clumsy request for an emergency collect call to the one number burned into his memory since age 14: his home.

The track captures the agonizing wait as the Relay Operator connects the line. The emotional climax hits not with a musical crescendo, but with a single spoken word sampled from the "Black Box" recording of the call: **Stella accepting the charges.**

**LYRICS:**

[Instrumental]

(The track opens with the harsh, rhythmic *skree-skree* of a TTY handshake connection. It acts as the percussion track.)

(A nervous, staccato synthesizer melody plays, mimicking Leo's hesitant one-finger typing. He ignores the "GA" prompts, just typing a continuous stream of panic.)

(The music drops to a tense silence, broken only by the "ringing" tone.)

[Spoken Sample: Relay Operator]
(Heavily processed, sounding like a distant transmission)
"I have a collect call from... Leo. Will you accept the charges?"

(A beat of silence.)

[Spoken Sample: Stella]
(Clear, frantic, and immediate)
"YES."

(The music instantly shifts from cold industrial anxiety to a warm, swelling major-key pad. The connection is made. The nightmare is over.)

[Outro]
(The sound of rapid typing—Leo pouring his heart out via HCO (Hearing Carry Over)—blends with the warm synth, fading into the next track.)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/2-04-my-anchor-holds.md ---

**LORE NOTE:**
The emotional climax of the saga, but not the instant relief one might expect.

**Part 1: The Longest Hour (Instrumental)**
Leo has made the TTY call. He knows Olivia is coming. But she is in Lynnwood, a 45-minute taxi ride away. This instrumental captures the agonizing wait. Built on a nervous, pulsing synth-bass, it is layered with the distant sounds of a busy terminal. Leo is terrified that Delores might find him before Olivia does.

**Part 2: My Anchor (Reunion)**
The perspective shifts to **Olivia** (Maya). She tears through the terminal and spots him—shivering in his soaked sweatshirt, alone in the crowd.

**The Sonic Conflict:**
Crucially, the "Industrial" static of Disc 1 **does not** instantly vanish. The "Painted Smile" mask is still locked tight on Leo's face; his defenses are on red alert, fearing this is another one of Delores's tricks.
* **The Sound:** You hear the warm, soaring **80s Power Ballad** melody (Olivia/Safety) desperately trying to break through the mix, but it is constantly battered and suppressed by the grinding **Industrial** beat (Trauma/Delores).
* **The Experience:** It sounds like a radio signal fighting through jamming frequency. Leo *sees* her, but he can't let the wall down yet. The "80s" sound only fully conquers the "Industrial" noise in the final, soaring guitar solo—the moment he finally allows himself to be held.

--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/2-05-airside-sanctuary.md ---

**LORE NOTE:**
A gentle, shimmering synth-pop ballad set in the secure zone of **SEA-TAC Airport**.

The "Airside" represents the first true safe soil. Leo has made it through TSA. The threat of Delores is physically behind the security checkpoint. Now, the healing begins.

**1. The Restoration:** Olivia takes Leo to a family restroom—not to scream at him, but to restore his dignity. She helps him change out of his soaked, filthy clothes into a brand new, dry sweatshirt and sweatpants she bought at a terminal shop. The sensory relief of dry cotton on his skin is treated as a religious experience in the lyrics.

**2. The "Anti-Pasco" Moment:** The pivotal scene occurs when Olivia needs to use the restroom herself. Knowing Leo is too terrified to be left alone, she asks him a question Delores never did: **"Is this okay?"** She asks for his permission. It is a small act that returns a massive amount of agency to him.

**3. The View:** The track ends with them sitting by the window, Leo’s wheelchair positioned so he can watch the planes take off. He is clean, he is fed, he is held, and for the first time in weeks, he is watching the world move without fear.

**LYRICS:**

[Verse 1]
The water runs, the towel is warm
Washing off the lingering storm
The heavy scent of diesel fumes
 Replaced by soap in quiet rooms
"Lift your arms," a gentle sound
My feet are hovering off the ground
The wet and cold are peeled away
To face the light of this new day

[Chorus]
In the Airside Sanctuary
The world is soft and light
No more shadows, no more scary
Fragments of the night
I'm clean, I'm dry, I'm safe, I'm here
Watching the gray clouds disappear
Airside...
Safe inside...

[Verse 2]
The door is locked, but I'm not trapped
The map of hell has been re-mapped
You turn to me, you meet my eyes
No anger there, no heavy sighs
"Do you mind?" you softly ask
Removing one more heavy mask
You give me space, you give me choice
You give me back my stolen voice

[Chorus]
In the Airside Sanctuary
The world is soft and light
No more shadows, no more scary
Fragments of the night
I'm clean, I'm dry, I'm safe, I'm here
Watching the gray clouds disappear

--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/2-06-homeward-on-this-day.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics
// FILE:      2-06-homeward-on-this-day.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/2-06-homeward-on-this-day.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   137
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
The album's great irony.

Most of the world knows this song as the massive holiday hit by **The Arctic Symphony Project** (a pseudonym Mirage used for their 2002 album *Northern Star Requiem*). That version—soaring, orchestral, and festive—was the soundtrack to the winter of 2002 and was still in heavy rotation when Leo sat in SEA-TAC Airport in **November 2003**. Hearing that bright, hopeful version over the terminal PA is what gave him the strength to believe he might actually make it home.

But on *this* album (released 2001), we hear the **original**: dark, moody, and desperate. This is the song as it was written in the midst of the trauma—a heavy, industrial prayer for rescue.

**Label Note:** Engine Room Records did not mandate the holiday album. Julian and Maya chose to re-record the song in 2002 as an act of reclamation—transforming their darkest moment into a source of pure, commercial joy.

**LYRICS:**

[Intro]
(Starts with a dramatic, slightly melancholic piano melody, soon joined by swelling orchestral strings that build anticipation)

[Verse 1]
A thousand miles of winter gray
Another dawn, another day
The engine's hum, a weary soul
A heavy price, a heavy toll
I see your face on frozen glass
A memory that's built to last
A single light that I must find
To leave the endless dark behind

[Chorus]
And the lights are bright, on this winter's night
Because you're coming home
Yes, you're finally coming home
Through the wind and rain, through the doubt and pain
You are not alone
No, you're finally coming home!

[Verse 2]
The towns fly by, a blur of light
I count the hours in the night
Remembering a whispered vow
It feels so close, it's coming now
The fire's lit, the table's set
A promise that I haven't met
But in my heart, the feeling grows
A warmth that melts the winter snows

[Chorus]
And the lights are bright, on this winter's night
Because you're coming home
Yes, you're finally coming home
Through the wind and rain, through the doubt and pain
You are not alone
No, you're finally coming home!

[Instrumental Bridge]
(Orchestra swells, strings become more frantic and dramatic)

[Guitar Solo]
(A powerful, melodic, and emotional electric guitar solo soars over the orchestra, telling the story of the struggle and the breakthrough)

[Chorus]
And the lights are bright, on this winter's night
Because you're coming home
Yes, you're finally coming home
Through the wind and rain, through the doubt and pain
You are not alone
No, you're finally coming home!

[Outro]
(Music becomes even more grand and layered with powerful choral vocals)
Coming home!
(Finally coming home!)
On this day!
(The orchestra and guitar build to a final, triumphant chord and fade)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/2-07-a-song-of-homecoming.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics
// FILE:      2-07-a-song-of-homecoming.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/2-07-a-song-of-homecoming.md

// TRACK NO:   143
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
A driving, mid-tempo rock anthem that balances anxiety with ascending hope.

The setting is the **Gate Area** at SEA-TAC. Leo and Olivia are waiting for **Gateway Airlines**. Because the airline has no assigned seating, a chaotic, aggressive queue is forming, triggering Leo's sensory overload. He braces himself to be crushed in the rush, terrified he will be separated from Olivia.

**The Pre-Boarding Revelation:**
Leo doesn't know that his disability gives him the right to pre-board. He expects a fight. Instead, Olivia calmly guides him past the line. The Gate Agent doesn't stop them; she smiles and waves them through before anyone else. The realization that he has rights—that he isn't just cargo—is the spark that finally ignites the "Homecoming" chorus.

**The Internal Conflict:**
Leo holds his boarding pass with a death grip. His "Painted Smile" mask is still partially engaged; his eyes dart around the terminal, half-expecting Delores (Vanessa) to burst through security and drag him back. He cannot fully believe he is leaving until the wheels actually leave the ground.

**The Sound:**
The track features a nervous, staccato guitar riff (the anxiety of the crowd) that slowly opens up into a wide, majestic chorus (the quiet safety of the jetway). The "Industrial" noise of the city is still present in the verses—hissing steam, mechanical clatter—but the chorus is pure, sweeping 80s optimism, representing Olivia's steady presence guiding him home.

**LYRICS:**

[Verse 1]
The monitor flickers, the crowd begins to sway
I'm bracing for the crush, the price I have to pay
The noise is getting louder, the air is getting thin
I'm terrified of losing you amidst the din
But then you touch my shoulder, you guide me past the line
"We're going first," you whisper, "This moment is all mine"
The agent scans the paper, the heavy door swings wide
Leaving all the chaos and the angry noise outside

[Chorus]
And I hear a song of homecoming
Underneath the fear, a steady humming
A melody that's calling me away
To the light of a brand new day
It's faint and fragile, but it's true
I'm walking down this hall with you
A song of homecoming...
Please let it be true...

[Verse 2]
The tunnel is so quiet, the carpet soft and gray
A silence that I haven't heard for many, many days
I check behind me, trembling, just one last time
Searching for the punishment, searching for the crime
But there's only empty hallway and the echo of our feet
And the promise of a window seat
You help me to the transfer, you buckle me in tight
For the first time in forever, I'm ready for the flight

[Chorus]
And I hear a song of homecoming
Underneath the fear, a steady humming
A melody that's calling me away
To the light of a brand new day
It's faint and fragile, but it's true
I'm walking down this hall with you
A song of homecoming...
Please let it be true...

[Bridge]
(The music swells. The industrial clatter fades completely, replaced by the rising, high-pitched whine of jet turbines spooling up.)
The cabin pressure, the safety sign
For the first time in months, this life is mine
You take my hand, you squeeze it tight
We're lifting off into the night!

[Outro]
(The drums kick into a double-time beat—the takeoff roll.)
We're going home!
We're going home!
Don't look back!
We're going home!
(Fade out on the roar of engines)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/2-08-flight-1138-sanctuary-in-the-sky.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics
