// TRACK NO:   149
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
The "exhale" after four days of holding his breath. A soaring, weightless synth-pop anthem that captures the pure euphoria of escape.

The setting is **Gateway Airlines Flight 1138**, climbing out of Seattle en route to Chicago (MDW). Leo and Olivia are in **Row 2**. Leo watched the boarding door like a hawk until it thudded shut, confirming **Delores was not on board.**

**The "Safety" Beat:**
For the takeoff, Leo is rigid, following every FAA rule out of fear. It isn't until the plane breaks through the cloud layer and the **10,000-foot chime** rings that he finally breaks. The "ding" is his permission to let go. He reclines his seat, lowers the tray table, and curls into Olivia's shoulder. Today is the first time he has been touched with genuine, non-transactional love since the nightmare began.

**LYRICS:**

[Verse 1]
The engine hums a steady beat
Thirty thousand tired feet
Above the clouds, a sea of white
Escaping from the endless night
Your hand in mine, a steady grace
The only anchor in this place
You let me lean against your side
With nothing left to run or hide

[Chorus]
(Music swells with a warm, gentle, and melodic feel)
And for the first time in so long, I can breathe
A quiet promise, I believe
Your shoulder is my only home right now
A silent, true, and sacred vow
This sanctuary in the sky
Beneath a calm and endless eye
Is all I need to feel okay
And chase the lingering fog away

[Verse 2]
I think about the static sound
On that unholy, hollow ground
I think about the pouring rain
The endless fear, the burning pain
But then I feel you squeeze my hand
And I begin to understand
The storm has passed, the war is done
A quiet battle has been won

[Chorus]
(Music swells with a warm, gentle, and melodic feel)
And for the first time in so long, I can breathe
A quiet promise, I believe
Your shoulder is my only home right now
A silent, true, and sacred vow
This sanctuary in the sky
Beneath a calm and endless eye
Is all I need to feel okay
And chase the lingering fog away

[Instrumental Bridge]
(A melodic, slightly melancholic, but ultimately hopeful synthesizer solo, with clean, arpeggiated notes that sound like data flowing before fading out.)

[Outro]
(Music fades to just a low, humming drone, like the cabin of the plane. The vocals are a quiet, spoken-word whisper.)
My anchor...
Safe...
Thank you...


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/2-09-midway-to-norfolk-the-final-leg.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics
// FILE:      2-09-midway-to-norfolk-the-final-leg.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/2-09-midway-to-norfolk-the-final-leg.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   155
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
An upbeat, kinetic electronic instrumental that chronicles the final leg of the journey: **Flight 492 from Chicago Midway (MDW) to Norfolk (ORF)**.

After the terror of the bus stations and the white-knuckle tension of Sea-Tac, the transfer at Midway was miraculously simple. No screaming, no running, no "Pasco Lock" moments. Just Olivia guiding him calmly to the next gate, buying him a magazine, and boarding the plane.

The music reflects this new reality. The "Industrial" grit of Disc 1 is completely purged from the mix, replaced by clean, arpeggiated synthesizers and a steady, propulsive 125 BPM beat. It is the sound of a plane cutting through the night sky, carrying Leo back to the only people who truly know him. The mood is no longer about *escaping*; it is about *arriving*.

**LYRICS:**

[Instrumental]

(The track opens with a clean, rapid electronic drum beat at 125 BPM—fast, eager, and light.)

(A warm, melodic synthesizer bass line enters, driving the song forward with a sense of purpose. It feels like the heartbeat of someone running toward a finish line.)

(Layers of bright, arpeggiated synthesizers build on top, creating a "flying" sensation. The melody is major-key, hopeful, and ascending.)

(The bridge features a shimmering "starfall" sound effect, representing the lights of the East Coast appearing on the horizon.)

(The track ends not with a fade, but with a triumphant, sustained chord that resolves perfectly into the next track.)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/2-10-sanctuary.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics
// FILE:      2-10-sanctuary.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/2-10-sanctuary.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   161
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
The album's emotional climax. The setting is the arrival gate at **Norfolk International Airport (ORF)**.

When Leo rolls off the jetway and sees Stella and Luna waiting for him, the overwhelming relief triggers the most severe autistic meltdown of his life. It is a "System Crash" born of the unbearable reality that he is actually safe.

**The Promise:**
The most critical moment of the entire rock opera occurs in the second verse. **Luna (Isabella Croft)** delivers the line that finally breaks the trauma loop: **"Your broken heart will mend with time."**

This isn't a platitude. It is a binding vow from sister to brother. It is Luna using her voice to overwrite the damage Delores did, promising him that his current pain is temporary, but his family is permanent. When she sings this line, the "Painted Smile" mask doesn't just fall; it shatters, and Leo finally allows himself to be held.

**LYRICS:**

[Intro]
(The track opens with a wall of chaotic, industrial noise—distorted static, rushing wind, and a frantic, irregular heartbeat. It builds to an unbearable peak, then...)

(Silence.)

(A warm, gentle synth pad fades in. Clean, melodic electric guitar notes chime like bells.)

[Verse 1]
(Sung by Luna [Izzy Croft] - Clear, gentle, and unwavering)
The storm is over now, the static's gone
Right here is where you've been all along
Your voice was lost, your world was gray
We're here to chase the dark away
You walked through fire, you walked through rain
But you will never walk alone again

[Chorus]
We are your sanctuary in the storm
A fire here to keep you safe and warm
Inside this circle, held so tight
We'll be your true and gentle light
So let the painted smile fall down
You're standing on your native ground
You're home... you're finally home

[Verse 2]
The mask you wore is on the floor
You don't need it anymore
The silence has no power here
We'll banish every single fear
Just let it go, the heavy load
At the ending of this lonely road
Just lean on us, we'll hold the line
**Your broken heart will mend with time**

[Chorus]
We are your sanctuary in the storm
A fire here to keep you safe and warm
Inside this circle, held so tight
We'll be your true and gentle light
So let the painted smile fall down
You're standing on your native ground
You're home... you're finally home

[Guitar Solo]
(A soaring, melodic, and deeply emotional 80s power ballad guitar solo. It sounds like a release of breath held for four days.)

[Outro]
You're home... you're finally home
We are your sanctuary... you're not alone.
(Fades out on a warm, unresolved major chord)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/2-11-haven-city.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics
// FILE:      2-11-haven-city.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/2-11-haven-city.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   167
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
After the quiet, personal resolution of "Sanctuary," this track is the sound of pure, explosive, cathartic release.

**The Scene:**
Leo is in the backseat of Stella's car, sandwiched between Olivia and Luna. They are leaving the airport. Stella turns on the radio. The commercials end, and **this song** kicks in.

**The Sonic Shift:**
To represent Leo's internal state, the album version of this track begins with a "Fake-Out."
* **0:00 - 0:45:** It starts as a dark, heavy **Industrial** track—the lingering static of Delores's world.
* **The Break:** Suddenly, a clean, arpeggiated electric guitar riff cuts through the noise like a knife.
* **The Explosion:** The song erupts into a triumphant, unironic **Late-80s Hard Rock Anthem**. The industrial gloom vanishes, replaced by "Hair Metal" drums, soaring vocals, and the joy of a open highway.

For Leo, this isn't just a song; it is the sound of his authentic self returning. He is 19, he is safe, and he is finally, truly home.

**LYRICS:**

[Intro]
(The track opens with a heavy, distorted industrial drum machine and pulsing synth-bass—tense and weary.)

(Suddenly, a clean, chorus-drenched electric guitar riff cuts through the noise.)

(A massive, live drum fill kicks the song into high gear. The industrial sounds vanish instantly.)

[Verse 1]
Been on the road for a million miles
Seen a thousand tears and a thousand fake smiles
This engine's screamin', the diesel's burnin' low
Got nowhere left that I can go
I'm tired of the fightin', sick of the lies
Just wanna see my own familiar skies

[Chorus]
Oh, won't you take me home to Haven City!
Where the harbor lights are burning pretty!
Oh, won't you please just take me home, yeah!
Yeah, take me home to Haven City!
Leave the broken past for all to pity!
Oh, won't you please just take me home!

[Verse 2]
See the harbor lights up through the mist
A promise that I can't resist
Heard a kind voice on the telephone
Said 'boy, it's time that you came on home'
I'm done with darkness, done with pain
Standing out in the pouring rain

[Chorus]
Oh, won't you take me home to Haven City!
Where the harbor lights are burning pretty!
Oh, won't you please just take me home, yeah!
Yeah, take me home to Haven City!
Leave the broken past for all to pity!
Oh, won't you please just take me home!

[Guitar Solo]
(A soaring, melodic, and virtuosic late-80s hard rock guitar solo with massive bends and a triumphant, "stadium" feel.)

[Outro - Double Time]
(The tempo kicks up into a driving double-time rhythm)
Take me home!
(Oh, won't you please take me home!)
Take me home!
(To the Haven City!)
Take me home!
(Yeah!)
Take me home!
(To the harbor lights burning pretty!)
Take me home! HOME!
(Music ends with a final, crashing power chord and feedback)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/2-12-guiding-stars-reprise.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics
// FILE:      2-12-guiding-stars-reprise.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/2-12-guiding-stars-reprise.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   173
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
The thematic epilogue of *Static & Silence*.

This track serves as a direct response to the album opener, "Nineteen and Invincible." In that first song, a naive Leo sang about his "three guiding stars" (Stella, Luna, and Olivia) with innocent optimism. Now, after surviving the industrial nightmare of Disc 1, he returns to that melody with a profound, weary gratitude.

He realizes that his sisters and best friend weren't just "guiding" him; they were the only things that kept him alive. The lyrics explicitly reference the trauma mechanisms he developed ("I learned to wear a painted smile / And walk a cold and lonely mile"), acknowledging the scars that will remain. But the music—a calm, sophisticated 80s pop arrangement featuring a soulful saxophone—signals that while the innocence is gone, the safety is permanent. He vows never to leave the Fortress again.

**LYRICS:**

[Verse 1]
There was a boy I used to be
Who saw the world so simply
Thought every road was straight and true
Beneath a sky of perfect blue
He thought that love was just a phrase
For sunny, easy, golden days
He called you all his guiding stars
Before he knew about the scars

[Chorus]
But I learned the sky can fall away
And turn a bright and sunny day to gray
And in the static and the night
You were my only guiding light
More than a feeling, more than a name
You were the fire, you were the flame
That led me through the pouring rain
And brought me back to life again

[Verse 2]
I learned to wear a painted smile
And walk a cold and lonely mile
To build a wall of industrial sound
On cold and unfamiliar ground
I learned how silence has a weight
And how a heart can fill with hate
I learned that trust can be a lie
Beneath a cold and empty sky

[Bridge]
Two stars are here, held close and tight
One shines a warm and distant light
A friendly face on a glowing screen
The miles can't ever come between
The bond we made, the truth we know
Helping a broken boy to grow

[Chorus]
And when the sky would fall away
And turn a bright and sunny day to gray
And in the static and the night
You were my only guiding light
More than a feeling, more than a name
You were the fire, you were the flame
That led me through the pouring rain
And brought me back to life again

[Instrumental Outro]
(A gentle, melodic, and deeply soulful saxophone solo plays, full of quiet reflection and gratitude. It weaves around the main melody, lingering like a long exhale, as the music slowly fades to silence.)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/2-13-ill-drag-you-back.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics
// FILE:      2-13-ill-drag-you-back.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/2-13-ill-drag-you-back.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   179
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
A fierce, protective power ballad sung entirely by **Luna (Isabella Croft)**.

This is the "Warrior Sister" anthem. Having nearly lost her twin brother to the silence of Delores, Luna makes a binding vow: she will never let him drift away again. The lyrics are not just a comfort; they are a declaration of intent. If the darkness comes back, she won't just call his name—she will physically **drag** him back to safety.

**The Sonic Duel:**
In a unique production choice, the track features a duel between a heavy, distorted electric guitar (representing the threat/trauma) and a piercing, aggressive **Flute** (representing Luna).

The guitar tries to dominate the mix, but the flute weaves through the distortion, matching it note-for-note in intensity and volume. It is a sonic metaphor for Luna's promise: she isn't afraid of the noise anymore. She will cut through any static to find him.

**LYRICS:**

[Verse 1]
I saw the static in your eyes
The silent screams, the practiced lies
A painted smile, a hollow shell
Trapped inside a private hell
I tried to call, I tried to warn
On that cold and broken morn
You couldn't hear my quiet plea
But you are finally back with me

[Chorus]
And I will never let you go again
This is a promise, not a "what if" or a "when"
If the darkness ever calls your name
I'll be the fire, I'll be the flame
And if I have to, with my own two hands
I'll drag you back from those broken lands

[Verse 2]
I'll be the flute song in your head
For every single word unsaid
A steady note, a gentle sound
On this new and solid ground
No more silence, no more fear
As long as I am standing here
You'll never have to walk alone
I'll bring my broken brother home

[Chorus]
And I will never let you go again
This is a promise, not a "what if" or a "when"
If the darkness ever calls your name
I'll be the fire, I'll be the flame
And if I have to, with my own two hands
I'll drag you back from those broken lands

[Instrumental Solo]
(A fierce, technical duel between the electric guitar and the flute. The guitar growls with distortion, but the flute soars above it, piercing and undeniable, ultimately claiming the final, sustained high note.)

[Chorus]
And I will never let you go again
This is a promise, not a "what if" or a "when"
If the darkness ever calls your name
I'll be the fire, I'll be the flame
And if I have to, with my own two hands
I'll drag you back from those broken lands


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/2-14-the-scar-remains.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics
// FILE:      2-14-the-scar-remains.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/2-14-the-scar-remains.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   185
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
A mature, reflective rock track that serves as the thematic resolution of the album.

If "Sanctuary" was the relief of rescue, **"The Scar Remains"** is the hard work of recovery. Leo acknowledges that he cannot simply "reset" to the boy he was in Track 1 ("Nineteen and Invincible"). That version of him is gone, lost to the "Long Road West."

Instead of fighting this loss, he accepts it. The lyrics describe the slow, non-linear process of learning to trust again—not just his family, but his own judgment.

**The Sonic Synthesis:**
Musically, this track represents the complete integration of the band's sound. It features **Luna's Flute** (representing her emotional promise) weaving seamlessly with **Stella's New Wave Synthesizer** (representing her structural protection), both grounded by a steady, classic 80s rock beat. It is the sound of his support system becoming part of his own internal rhythm.

**LYRICS:**

[Verse 1]
I'm trying to find that boy I knew
In shades of brilliant white and blue
The one who thought he had it made
Before the colors start to fade
He's still in here, a distant ghost
The one I miss and fear the most
A part of me is lost, it's true
A space that was reserved for you... Olivia

[Chorus]
But the scars remain, a map to trace
And they have found their proper place
I'm standing on a different floor
Than I was standing on before
And I can hear a brand new sound
On this new and solid ground
The echo of a worried flute
A synthesizer's loving root

[Verse 2]
I'm learning how to trust again
To let the gentle sunlight in
I'm learning that a helping hand
Can help a broken boy to stand
I'm learning that a home can be
A fortress built for only me
By two strong women, brave and true
Who know exactly what to do

[Chorus]
'Cause the scars remain, a map to trace
And they have found their proper place
I'm standing on a different floor
Than I was standing on before
And I can hear a brand new sound
On this new and solid ground
The echo of a worried flute
A synthesizer's loving root

[Instrumental Outro]
(The flute and synthesizer trade melodies, harmonizing over the rock beat before fading into a warm, sustained chord.)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/2-15-this-house-stands.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics
// FILE:      2-15-this-house-stands.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/mirage/2001-static-and-silence/lyrics/2-15-this-house-stands.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   191
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
The album's finale. A powerful, reassuring vow sung by **Stella (Beatrice Croft)**.

Throughout the album, Leo has been the one searching for safety. In this final track, Stella confirms that the search is over. She directly addresses the specific traumas Delores inflicted—the "shifting sands" of gaslighting, the "slamming doors" of isolation, and the "cage" of non-consensual touch.

She counters each trauma with a permanent architectural promise: **"This House Stands."** It is a declaration that their family unit is a fortress that will never break. She swears that he will never face abuse again, not because the world is safe, but because *she* is standing between him and the world.

**LYRICS:**

[Verse 1]
You learned to live in shifting sands
With no safe place to ever land
You learned that doors could slam and lock
Against the ticking of the clock
You learned that touch could be a cage
A quiet, simmering, bitter rage
But that old world is dead and gone
It's time to face a brand new dawn

[Chorus]
So let my arms be your new walls
To catch you every time you fall
This house stands, it will not break
For your own precious, gentle sake
If it's a hug that you should need
A simple, pure, and loving creed
Then I am here, my love is true
My arms will be a home for you

[Verse 2]
No need to brace, no need to hide
With your two sisters by your side
We'll be the lock, we'll be the key
To your own safe reality
We'll be the roof against the rain
To wash away the hurt and pain
This is a vow we'll always keep
While you are in your troubled sleep

[Guitar Solo]
(A clean, melodic, and triumphant guitar solo. It doesn't shred; it builds a steady, unbreakable wall of sound.)

[Chorus]
So let my arms be your new walls
To catch you every time you fall
This house stands, it will not break
For your own precious, gentle sake
If it's a hug that you should need
A simple, pure, and loving creed
Then I am here, my love is true
My arms will be a home for you

[Outro]
This house stands...
For you...
(Final sustained chord, resolving perfectly into silence)

--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics/1-01-electric-color.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics
// FILE:      1-01-electric-color.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics/1-01-electric-color.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   3
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
The solid **#2 hit**. It sat at the runner-up spot for six weeks, famously blocked from #1 by a certain "novelty song" that swept the summer of '87.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
Woke up this morning, the world was gray
Didn't know the colors had all faded away
Been living in a room with the curtains drawn tight
Then you flipped a switch and turned on the light

(Chorus)
Oh, I'm seeing in electric color
Finding a rhythm I never knew
Dancing in a brand new summer
My whole world feels bright and new
It's an electric-colored feeling
And it all started with you!

(Verse 2)
The city sounds were just a buzz in my ear
A static station I could never quite hear
But now the noise is turning into a song
And I finally feel like I belong

(Chorus)
Oh, I'm seeing in electric color
Finding a rhythm I never knew
Dancing in a brand new summer
My whole world feels bright and new
It's an electric-colored feeling
And it all started with you!

(Bridge)
The beat is getting stronger
I can't hide it any longer
My heart is playing catch-up
To the music turning way up!

(Guitar Solo)

(Chorus)
Oh, I'm seeing in electric color
Finding a rhythm I never knew
Dancing in a brand new summer
My whole world feels bright and new
It's an electric-colored feeling
And it all started with you!

(Outro)
Electric color
Feeling so new
It's all because of you


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics/1-02-plastic-sky.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics
// FILE:      1-02-plastic-sky.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics/1-02-plastic-sky.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   4
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
Ryan's first act of "malicious compliance." He hid angsty, anti-corporate lyrics about "living under a plastic sky" inside a polished, studio-approved pop-rock wrapper. It is a direct protest against the "thin" sound the studio demanded—Ryan sings explicitly about his guitar being "clean" and the drums being "thin" while the executives nodded along, missing the insult completely.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
The camera's on, the smile is bright
Another take in the burning light
They painted the set with a perfect blue
But it doesn't feel real, it doesn't feel true

(Chorus)
We're living under a plastic sky (Oh-oh-oh)
They tell me "jump," I ask "how high?"
(Oh-oh-oh)
It's just a set, it's all a lie
Living under a plastic sky

(Verse 2)
My guitar is clean, the drums are thin
They polished away all the grit and sin
I want to scream, I want to roar
But they just ask me to sing some more

(Chorus)
We're living under a plastic sky (Oh-oh-oh)
They tell me "jump," I ask "how high?"
(Oh-oh-oh)
It's just a set, it's all a lie
Living under a plastic sky

(Bridge)
Is this the dream we were chasing?
A different part, a different face
I'm losing me, I'm losing you
In all this shiny, plastic blue

(Guitar Solo)

(Chorus)
We're living under a plastic sky (Oh-oh-oh)
They tell me "jump," I ask "how high?"
(Oh-oh-oh)
It's just a set, it's all a lie
Living under a plastic sky

(Outro)
Plastic sky...
Feeling so new...
(fades out)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics/1-03-my-anchor-holds.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics
// FILE:      1-03-my-anchor-holds.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics/1-03-my-anchor-holds.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   5
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
A power ballad the studio marketed as a romance, but which the band wrote about their family bond. This "sibling-love-disguised-as-romance" became a staple of their early sound.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
The lights are bright, but the room is cold
Another story that's bought and sold
I'm trying to find a reason why
Beneath this plastic, empty sky

(Chorus)
But when the world is spinning out of my control
You're the one true thing that I can hold
Through the static and the driving rain
You're the one who keeps me sane
You are the shelter, you are the truth
My anchor in this crazy youth

(Verse 2)
They pull us under, they push us high
They build us up just to watch us die
It's hard to know who you can trust
When all the promises turn to dust

(Chorus)
But when the world is spinning out of my control
You're the one true thing that I can hold
Through the static and the driving rain
You're the one who keeps me sane
You are the shelter, you are the truth
My anchor in this crazy youth

(Guitar Solo)

(Bridge)
So let them talk, let them stare
As long as you are standing there
I'm not afraid of what's to come
I know where I belong...

(Chorus)
'Cause when the world is spinning out of my control
You're the one true thing that I can hold
Through the static and the driving rain
You're the one who keeps me sane
You are the shelter, you are the truth
My anchor in this crazy youth

(Outro)
My anchor...
My anchor holds...
(Fades out with piano and synth)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics/1-04-dancing-in-the-neon.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics
// FILE:      1-04-dancing-in-the-neon.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics/1-04-dancing-in-the-neon.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   6
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
"I wanna see you dancing to this one, Chicago!" A crowd-pleaser that fit the label's "pop" image perfectly. This was one of the "safe" synth-pop singles Apex Records pushed for, prioritizing high energy and polished production over the band's rock roots.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
The city's quiet, the sun is down
We're in the glow of the other side of town
The beat is calling from a place I know
Just follow the sound, and let it go

(Chorus)
We're dancing in the neon (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
We're moving 'til the night is gone (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Forget about tomorrow
We're shining like a light
Dancing in the neon tonight!

(Verse 2)
The rhythm's heavy, a flashing bright
Reflected colors in your eyes
I don't care what the real world wants
This moment's all that I could ever want

(Chorus)
We're dancing in the neon (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
We're moving 'til the night is gone (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Forget about tomorrow
We're shining like a light
Dancing in the neon tonight!

(Bridge)
Don't stop, don't stop the feeling
This music's got me reeling
Don't stop, don't stop the beat
(Synth-pop breakdown / keyboard solo)

(Chorus)
We're dancing in the neon (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
We're moving 'til the night is gone (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Forget about tomorrow
We're shining like a light
Dancing in the neon tonight!

(Outro)
Dancing in the neon... (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
'Til the night is gone...
(fades)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics/1-06-just-a-girl.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics
// FILE:      1-06-just-a-girl.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics/1-06-just-a-girl.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   8
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
Cassidy's quiet rebellion. Apex executives dismissed this moody, introspective track as "filler," but it became a grassroots anthem for female fans. The honest lyrics about her treatment in the industry ("They tell me 'sing the high note' / They tell me 'wear the red dress'") resonated deeply with young women who felt similarly boxed in by expectations.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
They tell me "sing the high note"
They tell me "wear the red dress"
They never ask me what I wrote
They only want to see the press

(Verse 2)
I bring my chords, my melody
They pat my head and disagree
They turn to him, "now *that's* a sound"
And I'm just standing in the background

(Chorus)
Am I ever gonna break through?
Am I ever gonna get my due?
Or am I just a girl in the band?
A pretty picture, holding your hand

(Verse 3)
They say "be 'Stardust', be the grace"
"Know your role and keep your place"
But I am more than what you see
I'm fighting just to be... me

(Chorus)
Am I ever gonna break through?
Am I ever gonna get my due?
Or am I just a girl in the band?
A pretty picture, holding your hand

(Bridge)
But the music's in my blood
The synth, the key, the flood
You can't erase the fire
My voice is getting higher

(Chorus)
Am I ever gonna break through?
Am I ever gonna get my due?
Or am I just a girl in the band?
A pretty picture, holding your hand

(Outro)
Just a girl...
Just a girl...
(fades out on a shimmering synth pad)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics/1-06-unning-on-the-edge.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics
// FILE:      1-06-unning-on-the-edge.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics/1-06-unning-on-the-edge.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
Another protest song. Ryan sings about being a "shadow in the light" and having his guitar suppressed ("This keyboard hook is all a bore / I know this guitar's made for more"). It features angsty, anti-corporate lyrics hidden inside a "safe" pop-rock wrapper.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
Another day, another show
They tell me which way the wind should blow
I'm smiling 'til my face is numb
Waiting for the day to come

(Pre-Chorus)
I can hear a different sound
A rumble starting underground
This pretty world is gonna break
How much more can I take?

(Chorus)
I'm running on the edge, about to lose control (Oh-oh-oh)
Trying to save my rock and roll soul
I'm running on the edge, a shadow in the light (Oh-oh-oh)
Gonna break out into the night!

(Verse 2)
This keyboard hook is all a bore
I know this guitar's made for more
I want to feel the fire and grit
I'm getting really sick of it

(Pre-Chorus)
I can hear a different sound
A rumble starting underground
This pretty world is gonna break
How much more can I take?

(Chorus)
I'm running on the edge, about to lose control (Oh-oh-oh)
Trying to save my rock and roll soul
I'm running on the edge, a shadow in the light (Oh-oh-oh)
Gonna break out into the night!

(Guitar Solo)

(Chorus)
I'm running on the edge, about to lose control (Oh-oh-oh)
Trying to save my rock and roll soul
I'm running on the edge, a shadow in the light (Oh-oh-oh)
Gonna break out into the night!

(Outro)
Running on the edge...
Running on the edge...
(fades)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics/1-07-light-up-the-night.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics
// FILE:      1-07-light-up-the-night.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics/1-07-light-up-the-night.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   9
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
The song Apex Records poured *all* of their money into. They were convinced this polished synth-pop track would be "The Big One." It wasn't. Despite the massive marketing campaign, the "safe" sound failed to connect with audiences in the way the band's authentic rock tracks did.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
I was walking in the shadows
I was living in the gray
Waiting for a signal
To help me find my way

(Pre-Chorus)
I can feel a new connection
Like a spark inside the dark
You're my one true direction
You're the fire, you're the spark

(Chorus)
We're gonna light up the night! (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Chasing the stars 'til the morning light
We're gonna make it all right (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Turning our color on, shining so bright
We're gonna light up the night!

(Verse 2)
This city sleeps, but we're awake
A promise that we'll never break
The future's ours, I see the sign
Just put your hand in mine

(Pre-Chorus)
I can feel a new connection
Like a spark inside the dark
You're my one true direction
You're the fire, you're the spark

(Chorus)
We're gonna light up the night! (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Chasing the stars 'til the morning light
We're gonna make it all right (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Turning our color on, shining so bright
We're gonna light up the night!

(Bridge)
No more shadows, no more gray
(Keyboard Solo)

(Chorus)
We're gonna light up the night! (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Chasing the stars 'til the morning light
We're gonna make it all right (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Turning our color on, shining so bright
We're gonna light up the night!

(Outro)
Light up the night... (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Shining so bright...
(fades)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics/1-08-break-the-walls.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics
// FILE:      1-08-break-the-walls.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics/1-08-break-the-walls.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   10
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
The one "pure rock" anthem Apex allowed on the album. It opened Side B and became an immediate fan-favorite for its raw energy. While the studio pushed "Light Up the Night," the fans embraced this track as the true spirit of the band.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
Been counting the days in this neon cage
Smiling for the cameras on a plastic stage
They turned the synths up, drowned the sound
But you can't keep a good thing down

(Chorus)
We're gonna break the walls tonight! (Yeah!)
Turn up the volume, hit the light!
No more whispers, time to shout
The Engine's roaring, let it out!

(Verse 2)
I'm tired of the glitter, tired of the gloss
Tired of the suits who think they're the boss
This guitar is loaded, it's ready to scream
Waking up from their pretty dream

(Chorus)
We're gonna break the walls tonight! (Yeah!)
Turn up the volume, hit the light!
No more whispers, time to shout
The Engine's roaring, let it out!

(Guitar Solo)
(A blistering, technical, shredding solo that Ryan fought to keep on the record)

(Bridge)
For every kid who feels the same
Trapped in a world, playing their game
This one's for you, this one's for me
Time to be free!

(Chorus)
We're gonna break the walls tonight! (Yeah!)
Turn up the volume, hit the light!
No more whispers, time to shout
The Engine's roaring, let it out!

(Outro)
Let it out!
Break the walls!
C'mon!
(fades on a hanging guitar chord)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics/1-09-worlds-apart.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics
// FILE:      1-09-worlds-apart.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics/1-09-worlds-apart.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   11
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
An epic mid-tempo rock ballad that defied the studio's attempt to separate the band's "Stardust" and "Engine" personas. It features a massive, soaring duet between Ryan and Cassidy, proving their voices worked best when fighting together rather than apart. The lyrics in the third verse ("They want the 'Stardust,' they want the 'Engine'") are a direct meta-commentary on their struggle with Apex Records.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
Another mile on a lonely road
Just trying to get back to you
I'm carrying this heavy load
They're telling me what I have to do

(Verse 2)
The lights are bright, the stage is cold
They're trying to fit me in their mold
I see your face across the black
I know that we can find our way back

(Chorus)
We're worlds apart, but our hearts beat the same (Oh-oh)
I can hear you call my name
Through the distance, through the static and the dark
We're finding a way back to the start (Oh-oh)
Even when we're worlds apart

(Verse 3)
They want the "Stardust," they want the "Engine"
A different version for a different show
But we're the ones who'll be here in the end
We've got a fire they'll never know

(Chorus)
We're worlds apart, but our hearts beat the same (Oh-oh)
I can hear you call my name
Through the distance, through the static and the dark
We're finding a way back to the start (Oh-oh)
Even when we're worlds apart

(Guitar Solo)

(Bridge)
And when the mountains fall
And when the oceans rise
I'll find you (I'll find you!)

(Chorus)
We're worlds apart, but our hearts beat the same (Oh-oh)
I can hear you call my name
Through the distance, through the static and the dark
We're finding a way back to the start (Oh-oh)
Even when we're worlds apart

(Outro)
Worlds apart...
(fades)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics/1-10-another-night.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics
// FILE:      1-10-another-night.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics/1-10-another-night.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   12
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
A "safe" filler track demanded by Apex to pad out the album with radio-friendly pop. Ryan was explicitly banned from playing on this track ("No guitar allowed" was written on the session sheet), leaving Cassidy isolated with just a synthesizer backing track. Her vocal performance is genuinely upset, but not because of a breakup—she was miserable recording without her brother in the studio.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
The clock on the wall is ticking slow
I'm watching the rain fall on the window
I pick up the phone, but I let it go
Just waiting for a voice I know

(Chorus)
It's just another night without you (Oh-oh)
Another night I've got to get through
The city's sleeping, but I'm awake
Another night, for goodness sake

(Verse 2)
The TV's on, but the sound is low
I've got no other place to go
The silence is a heavy sound
I wish that you were here right now

(Chorus)
It's just another night without you (Oh-oh)
Another night I've got to get through
The city's sleeping, but I'm awake
Another night, for goodness sake

(Bridge)
(A very simple, minimal, clean keyboard solo. No guitar allowed.)

(Chorus)
It's just another night without you (Oh-oh)
Another night I've got to get through
The city's sleeping, but I'm awake
Another night, for goodness sake

(Outro)
Another night...
Another night...
(fades out)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics/1-11-out-of-time.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics
// FILE:      1-11-out-of-time.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics/1-11-out-of-time.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   13
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
Ryan's punk-inspired vent. The 13-track album was too long for a standard vinyl LP, so Apex executives "gleefully" cut this short, 1:35 track from the original vinyl release to save space. They viewed it as "noise," but to Ryan, cutting his rawest song was a direct attempt to silence the "Engine."

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
Tick-tock goes the plastic clock
Another meeting on another block
They're selling me a future that I don't want
It's just the same old tired font

(Chorus)
I'm out of patience!
I'm out of time!
This ain't your product, this life is mine!
Out of patience!
Out of time!
Gonna break free, gonna cross the line!

(Verse 2)
You want another ballad, soft and slow
Another song for the radio
But I've got a fire burning in my hands
You'll never, ever understand

(Chorus)
I'm out of patience!
I'm out of time!
This ain't your product, this life is mine!
Out of patience!
Out of time!
Gonna break free, gonna cross the line!

(Guitar Solo)
(Short, fast, and chaotic. Pure punk-rock energy)

(Chorus)
I'm out of patience!
I'm out of time!
This ain't your product, this life is mine!
Out of patience!
Out of time!
Gonna break free, gonna cross the line!

(Outro)
Out of time!
(Song ends abruptly on a final, crashing chord)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics/1-12-rearview-mirror.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics
// FILE:      1-12-rearview-mirror.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics/1-12-rearview-mirror.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   14
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
A moody, atmospheric "driving song" that sets the stage for the album's finale. While Apex approved it for its polished synth-pop sound, Cassidy used the lyrics to express her feeling of being a "passenger" in her own career. The poignant line "With all this 'Stardust' left inside" serves as a double entendre for her suppressed creative potential vs. the manufactured image she was forced to project.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
Another city, another night
We're speeding past the exit light
I see the skyline start to fade
A memory of a choice I made

(Chorus)
I'm looking in the rearview mirror
The past is getting clearer
Was it all just a mistake?
A heart that's learning how to break
It's all in the rearview mirror

(Verse 2)
This neon glow feels cold and thin
I don't know where I'm supposed to begin
I'm just a passenger in this ride
With all this "Stardust" left inside

(Chorus)
I'm looking in the rearview mirror
The past is getting clearer
Was it all just a mistake?
A heart that's learning how to break
It's all in the rearview mirror

(Bridge)
(A moody, atmospheric keyboard solo)
I'm driving fast, but I'm not free
Just running from the girl I used to be

(Chorus)
I'm looking in the rearview mirror
The past is getting clearer
Was it all just a mistake?
A heart that's learning how to break
It's all in the rearview mirror

(Outro)
Fading... fading...
In the rearview mirror...
(fades)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics/1-13-the-stardust-engine.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics
// FILE:      1-13-the-stardust-engine.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1987-electric-color/lyrics/1-13-the-stardust-engine.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   15
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
Buried at the end of the album, this is the band's true anthem. It is the only track on the debut that perfectly blends the "Stardust" (synths) and "Engine" (rock) identities. Critics and chart historians agree: had Apex released this as the lead single instead of burying it, it would have spent **11 straight weeks at #1**, dethroning the existing record holder for the decade.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
We were looking for a signal
A light in the endless black
A single voice in the universe
To tell us we're on the right track

(Verse 2)
We found the fire in the wires
We found the heart in the machine
Building a dream from nothing
A sound the world has never seen

(Chorus)
We are the Stardust Engine!
Racing through the night
We're burning on the fuel of a brand new light
We are the Stardust Engine!
Yeah, we're taking flight
Turning our future on, shining so bright!

(Verse 3)
They tried to ground us, keep us small
Tell us that we were gonna fall
But they don't know the things we know
They haven't seen the places we'll go

(Chorus)
We are the Stardust Engine!
Racing through the night
We're burning on the fuel of a brand new light
We are the Stardust Engine!
Yeah, we're taking flight
Turning our future on, shining so bright!

(Guitar Solo)
(A soaring, melodic solo that trades licks with a bright synthesizer hook)

(Bridge)
We are the cosmos, we are the fire
We're taking this one, higher and higher
We are the Stardust!
(We are the Engine!)
We are the Stardust Engine!

(Chorus)
We are the Stardust Engine!
Racing through the night
We're burning on the fuel of a brand new light
We are the Stardust Engine!
Yeah, we're taking flight
Turning our future on, shining so bright!

(Outro)
Shining so bright...
We're taking flight...
(fades out on an epic, sustained guitar chord)

--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1989-neon-hearts/lyrics/1-01-neon-hearts.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1989-neon-hearts/lyrics
// FILE:      1-01-neon-hearts.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1989-neon-hearts/lyrics/1-01-neon-hearts.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   3
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
The "safe" title track. Apex forced the band to include a callback lyric to their first hit ("It's an electric-colored feeling") to ensure brand consistency. The studio demanded this track open the album to assure fans that the "Stardust" sound hadn't changed, despite the band's desire to move toward rock.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
The city's humming, the lights are low
I'm waiting for you, I'm ready to go
The rain is tapping on the glass
I knew this feeling was built to last

(Pre-Chorus)
I hear your footsteps in the hall
I'm not afraid to fall
This is the only thing I know is true

(Chorus)
You've got my neon heart
Shining for you in the dark
A love so bright, it's off the charts
You've got my neon heart
It's an electric-colored feeling
And it all starts with you

(Verse 2)
We're walking fast on the wet concrete
The rhythm matches when our eyes meet
Don't need a promise, don't need a vow
My only moment is right here, right now

(Pre-Chorus)
I hear your footsteps in the hall
I'm not afraid to fall
This is the only thing I know is true

(Chorus)
You've got my neon heart
Shining for you in the dark
A love so bright, it's off the charts
You've got my neon heart
It's an electric-colored feeling
And it all starts with you

(Bridge)
(A bright, energetic keyboard solo)
Let the city sleep, we're wide awake
A chance that we're both gonna take!

(Chorus)
You've got my neon heart
Shining for you in the dark
A love so bright, it's off the charts
You've got my neon heart
It's an electric-colored feeling
And it all starts with you

(Outro)
My neon heart... (Shining for you)
My neon heart...
(fades out)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1989-neon-hearts/lyrics/1-02-hollow-sound.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1989-neon-hearts/lyrics
// FILE:      1-02-hollow-sound.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1989-neon-hearts/lyrics/1-02-hollow-sound.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   4
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
Ryan's act of "malicious compliance." He delivered the requested synth-pop sound but made the track **5:31 long**—knowing it was too long for radio—and included the protest line, "They're all afraid of the burning cross" (a reference to the canceled "Promise" single). [cite_start]It stands as a middle finger to the studio's "safe" direction[cite: 346, 347].

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
The red light's on, the tape is rolling
They're telling me what words to say
I'm trying to find the man I was
But he gets farther every day

(Verse 2)
They want the polish, they want the gleam
The "safer" version of the dream
But all I hear is a thin machine
Another part of their big scene

(Chorus)
It's just a hollow sound
My voice is in the mix
But the words are not my own
It's just a hollow sound
Another plastic fix
I'm singing all alone

(Verse 3)
They're all afraid of the burning cross
Terrified of what they lost
So now they're sanding off the edge
Pushing me back from the ledge

(Chorus)
It's just a hollow sound
My voice is in the mix
But the words are not my own
It's just a hollow sound
Another plastic fix
I'm singing all alone

(Bridge)
(A very clean, melodic, studio-approved keyboard solo)

(Chorus)
It's just a hollow sound
My voice is in the mix
But the words are not my own
It's just a hollow sound
Another plastic fix
I'm singing all alone

(Outro)
A hollow sound...
Just a hollow sound...
(fades)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1989-neon-hearts/lyrics/1-03-side-by-side.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1989-neon-hearts/lyrics
// FILE:      1-03-side-by-side.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1989-neon-hearts/lyrics/1-03-side-by-side.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   5
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
The studio's furious overcorrection to "Hollow Sound." After Ryan delivered a 5:31 protest track, Apex demanded an absurdly short, safe pop song to balance the runtime and "fix" the album's pacing. The result is this 1:54 "bubblegum" track that feels almost manic in its enforced cheerfulness.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
When the rain is falling
And you feel all alone
Just remember I'm here
You can call me on the phone

(Chorus)
'Cause we're always better together
In any kind of weather
We don't have to hide
We're walking side by side (Oh-oh)
Side by side

(Verse 2)
The world can be a lonely place
A fast and hurried, crazy race
But we can slow it down a while
It's always better when I see you smile

(Chorus)
'Cause we're always better together
In any kind of weather
We don't have to hide
We're walking side by side (Oh-oh)
Side by side

(Bridge)
(A very short, simple, melodic keyboard riff)
Through the highs, through the lows
That's just the way it goes

(Chorus)
'Cause we're always better together
In any kind of weather
We don't have to hide
We're walking side by side (Oh-oh)
Side by side

(Outro)
Side by side...
(fades quickly)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1989-neon-hearts/lyrics/1-04-if-you-walked-away.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1989-neon-hearts/lyrics
// FILE:      1-04-if-you-walked-away.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1989-neon-hearts/lyrics/1-04-if-you-walked-away.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   6
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
The "late-night radio" ballad Apex demanded to balance out the album's "weirdness." The studio marketed this as a romantic "breakup what-if" song. In reality, Cassidy sang it to Ryan. Isolated by the studio's attempts to separate their "Stardust" and "Engine" personas, this was her genuine expression of fear that he might actually leave her alone in the machine.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
The streetlights make a blurry line
I'm running through the same old signs
I'm just a face in a passing crowd
Afraid to speak my thoughts out loud

(Verse 2)
But then you found me in the noise
You heard a whisper in my voice
You gave me something to believe
A reason I would never leave

(Chorus)
If you walked away
The sky would lose its blue
I wouldn't know what to do
I'd be lost in the gray
If you walked away
My world would fall apart
You're the owner of my (Neon) heart

(Verse 3)
They build these walls so high and steep
With promises they never keep
But we have a fortress, strong and true
I'm safe as long as I have you

(Chorus)
If you walked away
The sky would lose its blue
I wouldn't know what to do
I'd be lost in the gray
If you walked away
My world would fall apart
You're the owner of my (Neon) heart

(Bridge)
(A very safe, melodic, clean keyboard solo)
I'm holding on, I'm holding tight
Don't let me go into the night...

(Chorus)
If you walked away
The sky would lose its blue
I wouldn't know what to do
I'd be lost in the gray
If you walked away
My world would fall apart
You're the owner of my (Neon) heart

(Outro)
Don't walk away...
(fades out on piano)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1989-neon-hearts/lyrics/1-05-same-old-song.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1989-neon-hearts/lyrics
// FILE:      1-05-same-old-song.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1989-neon-hearts/lyrics/1-05-same-old-song.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   7
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
Ryan's "Trojan Horse." The track opens with a bright, optimistic keyboard hook that promises a radio-friendly hit, but the lyrics are a scathing critique of the studio's formulaic songwriting process. Ryan delivered the exact "polished sheen" Apex demanded, but used it to sing about how "safe" and suffocating the production had become ("Turn on the keyboard, keep the guitar low").

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
They hand me the chords, they're all the same
A different title for the same old game
Turn on the keyboard, keep the guitar low
Just another track for the radio

(Chorus)
It's just the same old song (Oh-oh)
Another right to make a wrong
I'm singing words I don't believe
It's getting harder and harder to breathe
It's just the same old song

(Verse 2)
I'm dreaming of a feedback wail
A drum kit hit like a hammer on a nail
But all I get is this polished sheen
The safest sound you've ever seen

(Chorus)
It's just the same old song (Oh-oh)
Another right to make a wrong
I'm singing words I don't believe
It's getting harder and harder to breathe
It's just the same old song

(Bridge)
(A very clean, melodic, studio-approved guitar solo)
Is anybody listening?
Is anybody real?
Or do you just like the way it feels?

(Chorus)
It's just the same old song (Oh-oh)
Another right to make a wrong
I'm singing words I don't believe
It's getting harder and harder to breathe
It's just the same old song

(Outro)
The same old song...
(fades out)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1989-neon-hearts/lyrics/1-06-chain-reaction.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1989-neon-hearts/lyrics
// FILE:      1-06-chain-reaction.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1989-neon-hearts/lyrics/1-06-chain-reaction.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   8
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
The start of the "Side B Rebellion." After an A-Side full of studio-mandated synth-pop, Apex allowed this "one-two punch" of pure rock (paired with the following track, "Not Your Doll") as a concession. It became an immediate fan favorite, proving that the "Engine" sound was what the audience actually wanted.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
Been counting the days in this neon cage
Smiling for the cameras on a plastic stage
They turned the synths up, drowned the sound
But you can't keep a good thing down

(Chorus)
We're gonna break the walls tonight! (Yeah!)
Turn up the volume, hit the light!
No more whispers, time to shout
The Engine's roaring, let it out!

(Verse 2)
I'm tired of the glitter, tired of the gloss
Tired of the suits who think they're the boss
This guitar is loaded, it's ready to scream
Waking up from their pretty dream

(Chorus)
We're gonna break the walls tonight! (Yeah!)
Turn up the volume, hit the light!
No more whispers, time to shout
The Engine's roaring, let it out!

(Guitar Solo)
(A blistering, technical, shredding solo that Ryan fought to keep on the record)

(Bridge)
For every kid who feels the same
Trapped in a world, playing their game
This one's for you, this one's for me
Time to be free!

(Chorus)
We're gonna break the walls tonight! (Yeah!)
Turn up the volume, hit the light!
No more whispers, time to shout
The Engine's roaring, let it out!

(Outro)
Let it out!
Break the walls!
C'mon!
(fades on a hanging guitar chord)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1989-neon-hearts/lyrics/1-07-not-your-doll.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1989-neon-hearts/lyrics
// FILE:      1-07-not-your-doll.md

// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   9
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
Cassidy's "fierce side" finally breaking through. Following immediately after "Chain Reaction," this arena rock anthem completes the "Side B Rebellion." In it, Cassidy directly rejects the "Stardust" persona Apex forced upon her. While the studio tried to suppress her "Engine" side, this track became the definitive proof that she could rock just as hard as her brother.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
You put me in a box, you told me to be sweet
The "Stardust" girl, the perfect beat
You told me "soften up," you told me "know your place"
But you never saw the fire in my face

(Chorus)
'Cause I'm not your doll!
I'm not your pretty toy
I'm not just a picture for a poster boy
I'm not your doll!
I'm here to make a choice
And I'm gonna make you hear my voice!

(Verse 2)
You're scared of the noise, you're scared of the grit
You're terrified that I'm not gonna fit
In the tiny world you built for me
But I'm about to set myself free

(Chorus)
'Cause I'm not your doll!
I'm not your pretty toy
I'm not just a picture for a poster boy
I'm not your doll!
I'm here to make a choice
And I'm gonna make you hear my voice!

(Guitar Solo)

(Bridge)
You heard the "story to be told"?
Well, I'm the one who'll break the mold!
You wanted 'Stardust'? You get the fire!
My voice is real, and it's getting higher!

(Chorus)
'Cause I'm not your doll!
I'm not your pretty toy
I'm not just a picture for a poster boy
I'm not your doll!
I'm here to make a choice
And I'm gonna make you hear my voice!

(Outro)
Not your doll!
Hear my voice!
(fades out on a wailing guitar note)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1989-neon-hearts/lyrics/1-08-quiet-on-the-set.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1989-neon-hearts/lyrics

// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   10
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
The "Punishment" begins. After the explosive rock energy of the "one-two punch" on Side B, Apex executives stepped in to "shut the Engine down." They forced Ryan to record this quiet, introspective ballad to kill the momentum. The lyrics are a meta-commentary on his silencing ("This is the price for being loud / Getting lost back in the crowd").

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
The "story" has been told
The echo's fading fast
I tried to "break the mold"
They told me it would never last
They're shutting down the "Engine"

(Chorus)
Now it's quiet on the set (Oh-oh)
Back to the silence and regret
This is the price for being loud
Getting lost back in the crowd
After the laughter...

(Verse 2)
The "noise" we made was all too real
They're terrified of what they feel
So turn the keyboards up again
And hide the anger and the pain

(Chorus)
Now it's quiet on the set (Oh-oh)
Back to the silence and regret
This is the price for being loud
Getting lost back in the crowd
After the laughter...

(Bridge)
(A soft, melodic keyboard solo. No guitars.)
They want the "Stardust," safe and light
Just close your eyes and end the fight
Just close your eyes...

(Chorus)
Now it's quiet on the set (Oh-oh)
Back to the silence and regret
This is the price for being loud
Getting lost back in the crowd
After the laughter...

(Outro)
After the laughter...
Just... quiet...
(fades out on a single piano note)s


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1989-neon-hearts/lyrics/1-09-shadow-dancing.md ---

// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   11
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
The studio strikes back. Immediately following the "rebellion" of Side B's opening tracks, Apex Records moved to crush the momentum. They forced Cassidy back into her "Stardust" persona for this moody, atmospheric synth track, explicitly banning electric guitars from the mix to ensure the album returned to a "safe, marketable" sound before the end.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
The city lights are cold tonight
I'm walking under the pale moonlight
I see two people passing by
Reflected in a stranger's eye

(Chorus)
We're just shadow dancing
A pantomime, a game of chance
Trying to make it look like romance
We're just shadow dancing
Afraid to stop, afraid to fall
Just shadows on the wall

(Verse 2)
You say the words I want to hear
You whisper "love" to hide the fear
We're both performing in this play
Hoping the night will turn to day

(Chorus)
We're just shadow dancing
A pantomime, a game of chance
Trying to make it look like romance
We're just shadow dancing
Afraid to stop, afraid to fall
Just shadows on the wall

(Bridge)
(A moody, atmospheric keyboard solo)
Is this real? I'll never know
Just follow where the music goes
Just keep on dancing...

(Chorus)
We're just shadow dancing
A pantomime, a game of chance
Trying to make it look like romance
We're just shadow dancing
Afraid to stop, afraid to fall
Just shadows on the wall

(Outro)
Shadows on the wall...
Shadow dancing...
(fades)


--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1989-neon-hearts/lyrics/1-10-after-the-show.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1989-neon-hearts/lyrics
// FILE:      1-10-after-the-show.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1989-neon-hearts/lyrics/1-10-after-the-show.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   12
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
Ryan's final laugh. As "punishment" for the heavy rock tracks earlier on Side B, Apex ordered him to close the album with a quiet ballad, explicitly forbidding "pounding drums" on the track sheet. [cite_start]Ryan complied by recording the song using **percussive acoustic guitar**—slapping the body of the instrument to simulate a beat[cite: 353]. It was a subtle, rhythmic "middle finger" to the executives who tried to silence the "Engine."

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
The stage is empty, the crowd is gone
Another night, another dawn
We played the part, we sang the song
And now we have to move along

(Verse 2)
The neon heart is turning low
It's just a faded, distant glow
Is this the dream we wanted so?
I guess we'll never know

(Chorus)
We're just echoes in the city
It's not so bad, it's not so pretty
Just echoes, turning to gray
We'll come back and fight another day

(Bridge)
(A soft, melodic acoustic guitar solo, underscored by the rhythmic slapping of the guitar body)
Another day...
Another way...

(Chorus)
We're just echoes in the city
It's not so bad, it's not so pretty
Just echoes, turning to gray
We'll come back and fight another day

(Outro)
After the show...
Goodnight...
(fades out on acoustic guitar)

--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1990-live-in-chicago/lyrics/1-01-the-stardust-engine-live-in-chicago.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1990-live-in-chicago/lyrics
// FILE:      1-01-the-stardust-engine-live-in-chicago.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1990-live-in-chicago/lyrics/1-01-the-stardust-engine-live-in-chicago.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   4
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
This track is the "Smoking Gun" that proves Julian Vance (the executive behind the *Friction* disaster) never did his homework. In the intro, Ryan O'Connell—who, like his sister, is autistic and values absolute clarity—explicitly introduces Cassidy as "my sister," publicly disproving the "married couple" assumption years before it destroyed their contract.

Musically, this recording is a source of fury for the band. It was the only concession Apex made—allowing them to play their self-titled anthem as the opener of their strict 30-minute support slot. However, the mix is "sabotaged": Cassidy's iconic "Stardust" synth leitmotif—the musical heart of the song—is buried so deep in the mix it is practically inaudible, leaving only the "Engine" rock elements.

**LYRICS:**

(Spoken Intro)
Hello Chicago! I'm Ryan O'Connell!
(Crowd cheers)
And this is my sister, Cassidy!
(Crowd cheers louder)
And we are The Stardust Engine!

(Verse 1)
We were looking for a signal
A light in the endless black
A single voice in the universe
To tell us we're on the right track

(Verse 2)
We found the fire in the wires
We found the heart in the machine
Building a dream from nothing
A sound the world has never seen

(Chorus)
We are the Stardust Engine!
Racing through the night
We're burning on the fuel of a brand new light
We are the Stardust Engine!
Yeah, we're taking flight
Turning our future on, shining so bright!

(Verse 3)
They tried to ground us, keep us small
Tell us that we were gonna fall
But they don't know the things we know
They haven't seen the places we'll go

(Chorus)
We are the Stardust Engine!
Racing through the night
We're burning on the fuel of a brand new light
We are the Stardust Engine!
Yeah, we're taking flight
Turning our future on, shining so bright!

(Guitar Solo)
(Ryan plays a blistering solo, but the "Call and Response" synth notes are frustratingly faint in the mix)

(Bridge)
We are the cosmos, we are the fire
We're taking this one, higher and higher
We are the Stardust!
(We are the Engine!)
We are the Stardust Engine!

(Chorus)
We are the Stardust Engine!
Racing through the night
We're burning on the fuel of a brand new light
We are the Stardust Engine!
Yeah, we're taking flight
Turning our future on, shining so bright!

(Outro)
Shining so bright...
We're taking flight...
(Spoken) Thank you!

**LORE NOTE:**
"Time is money!" With a strict 30-minute opening slot and a tour manager watching the clock, the band had no time for deep cuts. Apex Records demanded they prioritize their high-energy synth-pop hits to win over the headliner's pop-focused audience. You can hear the urgency in the performance—the tempo is slightly faster than the studio version as they race to fit the setlist into their allotted window.

We're in the glow of the other side of town

Just follow the sound, and let it go

Forget about tomorrow
We're shining like a light
Dancing in the neon tonight!

The rhythm's heavy, a flashing bright
Reflected colors in your eyes
I don't care what the real world wants
This moment's all that I could ever want

We're moving 'til the night is gone (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Forget about tomorrow
We're shining like a light
Dancing in the neon tonight!

Don't stop, don't stop the feeling
This music's got me reeling
Don't stop, don't stop the beat
(Synth-pop breakdown / keyboard solo)

(Chorus)
We're dancing in the neon (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
We're moving 'til the night is gone (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Forget about tomorrow
We're shining like a light
Dancing in the neon tonight!

Dancing in the neon... (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
'Til the night is gone...

--- START OF FILE raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1990-live-in-chicago/lyrics/1-04-if-you-walked-away-live-in-chicago.md ---
// DIRECTORY: raggiesoft-assets/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1990-live-in-chicago/lyrics
// FILE:      1-04-if-you-walked-away-live-in-chicago.md
// LIVE URL:  https://assets.raggiesoft.com/engine-room-records/artists/the-stardust-engine/1990-live-in-chicago/lyrics/1-04-if-you-walked-away-live-in-chicago.md
// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   22
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
For Cassidy, who is autistic, the lyrics of this song were not a performance—they were a terrifying "what-if" scenario that she experienced viscerally every time she sang it. While the studio marketed it as a romantic breakup ballad, Cassidy sang it as a desperate plea to her brother. **The fear in her voice on this recording is real.**

Ryan, who is also autistic, understood her need for absolute certainty better than anyone. He famously had to reassure her after *every single concert*—often using the exact same phrase, **"Family sticks together"**—to ground her back in reality and confirm that he was never going to leave her.

**Note: After this specific performance, Cassidy broke down in tears backstage. Ryan had to hold her in a deep pressure hug for twenty minutes to help her reset and remind her that he was right there.**

**LYRICS:**

(Spoken Intro)
Thank you. We're gonna slow things down for you for just a minute...

(Verse 1)
The streetlights make a blurry line
I'm running through the same old signs
I'm just a face in a passing crowd
Afraid to speak my thoughts out loud

(Verse 2)
But then you found me in the noise
You heard a whisper in my voice
You gave me something to believe
A reason I would never leave

(Chorus)
If you walked away
The sky would lose its blue
I wouldn't know what to do
I'd be lost in the gray
If you walked away
My world would fall apart
You're the owner of my (Neon) heart

(Verse 3)
They build these walls so high and steep
With promises they never keep
But we have a fortress, strong and true
I'm safe as long as I have you

(Chorus)
If you walked away
The sky would lose its blue
I wouldn't know what to do
I'd be lost in the gray
If you walked away
My world would fall apart
You're the owner of my (Neon) heart

(Bridge)
(A very safe, melodic, clean keyboard solo)
I'm holding on, I'm holding tight
Don't let me go into the night...

(Chorus)
If you walked away
The sky would lose its blue
I wouldn't know what to do
I'd be lost in the gray
If you walked away
My world would fall apart
You're the owner of my (Neon) heart

(Outro)
Don't walk away...
(Spoken) Thank you so much.

**LORE NOTE:**
The "Big Bet." Apex Records had poured their entire marketing budget into this track, convinced it was the #1 hit that would define the album. Consequently, the band was under immense pressure to deliver a "perfect," high-energy performance of it every night. You can hear the professional polish here—Cassidy hits every note and Ryan plays the "good soldier," executing the catchy hooks exactly as the studio demanded, proving they could play the game even if they hated the rules.

**LYRICS:**

(Spoken Intro)
Are you ready to light up the night, Chicago?!

(Verse 1)
I was walking in the shadows
I was living in the gray
Waiting for a signal
To help me find my way

(Pre-Chorus)
I can feel a new connection
Like a spark inside the dark
You're my one true direction
You're the fire, you're the spark

(Chorus)
We're gonna light up the night! (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Chasing the stars 'til the morning light
We're gonna make it all right (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Turning our color on, shining so bright
We're gonna light up the night!

(Verse 2)
This city sleeps, but we're awake
A promise that we'll never break
The future's ours, I see the sign
Just put your hand in mine

(Pre-Chorus)
I can feel a new connection
Like a spark inside the dark
You're my one true direction
You're the fire, you're the spark

(Chorus)
We're gonna light up the night! (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Chasing the stars 'til the morning light
We're gonna make it all right (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Turning our color on, shining so bright
We're gonna light up the night!

(Bridge)
No more shadows, no more gray
(Keyboard Solo)
Let's light it up!

(Chorus)
We're gonna light up the night! (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Chasing the stars 'til the morning light
We're gonna make it all right (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Turning our color on, shining so bright
We're gonna light up the night!

(Outro)
Light up the night... (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Shining so bright...
(Spoken) We love you!

// ALBUM INFO:  -  ()
// TRACK NO:   34
------------------------------------------------------------------
**LORE NOTE:**
A love letter between siblings disguised as a power ballad. While Apex Records marketed this as a romantic duet to sell records, the "Ironheads" in the audience knew the truth. When Ryan and Cassidy sang this to each other on stage, it wasn't about romance; it was about survival. It was their public promise that no matter what the studio threw at them, they would be each other's anchor in the storm.

**LYRICS:**

(Spoken Intro)
This is a song for that one person who keeps you sane... the one who's your anchor.

(Verse 1)
The lights are bright, but the room is cold
Another story that's bought and sold
I'm trying to find a reason why
Beneath this plastic, empty sky

(Chorus)
But when the world is spinning out of my control
You're the one true thing that I can hold
Through the static and the driving rain
You're the one who keeps me sane
You are the shelter, you are the truth
My anchor in this crazy youth

(Verse 2)
They pull us under, they push us high
They build us up just to watch us die
It's hard to know who you can trust
When all the promises turn to dust

(Chorus)
But when the world is spinning out of my control
You're the one true thing that I can hold
Through the static and the driving rain
You're the one who keeps me sane
You are the shelter, you are the truth
My anchor in this crazy youth

(Guitar Solo)
(A soaring, melodic solo that Ryan plays while standing back-to-back with Cassidy at the piano)

(Bridge)
So let them talk, let them stare
As long as you are standing there
I'm not afraid of what's to come
I know where I belong...

(Chorus)
'Cause when the world is spinning out of my control
You're the one true thing that I can hold
Through the static and the driving rain
You're the one who keeps me sane
You are the shelter, you are the truth
My anchor in this crazy youth

(Outro)
My anchor...
My anchor holds...
(Spoken) We love you, Chicago!

**LORE NOTE:**
The Grand Finale. Because The Stardust Engine was the opening act with a strict 30-minute slot, there was no "fake exit" or encore. They had to transition straight from the emotional weight of "My Anchor Holds" directly into this high-energy closer. The performance is frantic and joyful, designed to leave the audience buzzing for the headliner while proving that the "opening act" could steal the show.

**LYRICS:**

(Spoken Intro)
Thank you, Chicago! You've been beautiful!
This is our last song!

(Verse 1)
Woke up this morning, the world was gray
Didn't know the colors had all faded away
Been living in a room with the curtains drawn tight
Then you flipped a switch and turned on the light

(Chorus)
Oh, I'm seeing in electric color
Finding a rhythm I never knew
Dancing in a brand new summer
My whole world feels bright and new
It's an electric-colored feeling
And it all started with you!

(Verse 2)
The city sounds were just a buzz in my ear
A static station I could never quite hear
But now the noise is turning into a song
And I finally feel like I belong

(Chorus)
Oh, I'm seeing in electric color
Finding a rhythm I never knew
Dancing in a brand new summer
My whole world feels bright and new
It's an electric-colored feeling
And it all started with you!

(Bridge)
The beat is getting stronger
I can't hide it any longer
My heart is playing catch-up
To the music turning way up!

(Guitar Solo)

(Chorus)
Oh, I'm seeing in electric color
Finding a rhythm I never knew
Dancing in a brand new summer
My whole world feels bright and new
It's an electric-colored feeling
And it all started with you!

(Outro)
Electric color
Feeling so new
It's all because of you
(Spoken) Thank you! Goodnight, everybody! We're The Stardust Engine!
